## יאב (id: 002736000000000)

יאב
===

יאב (H2968)

verb

Wish

to desire, to long for \= process by which humans experience a more or less strong internal urge to know or do something

[PSA 119:131](https://ref.ly/Ps119:131)

Reinier de Blois; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 119:131


## יאה (id: 002737000000000)

יאה
===

יאה (H2969)

verb

Right

to be fitting \= state in which an event is considered appropriate

[JER 10:7](https://ref.ly/Jer10:7)

Reinier de Blois; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 10:7


## יַאֲזַנְיָה (id: 002738000000000)

יַאֲזַנְיָה
===========

יַאֲזַנְיָה (H2970 · H3153) — יַאֲזַנְיָהוּ, יְזַנְיָה, יְזַנְיָהוּ

name · אזן · יהוה

Names of People

**Collocation:** יַאֲזַנְיָה

Jaazaniah \= man; ◄ son of Jeremiah (not the prophet); ► in the prophet Jeremiah's time, head of Rechabite clan, who faithfully observed ancestral ways

[JER 35:3](https://ref.ly/Jer35:3)

Names of People

**Collocation:** יַאֲזַנְיָה

Jaazaniah \= man; ◄ son of Azzur; ► wicked leader of Jerusalem in time of Ezekiel

[EZK 11:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:1)

Names of People

**Collocation:** יַאֲזַנְיָהוּ · יְזַנְיָהוּ

Jaazaniah, Jezaniah \= man; ◄ son of the Maacathite (or from Maacah); ► army officer of Judah; time of exile

[2KI 25:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:23) · [JER 40:8](https://ref.ly/Jer40:8)

Names of People

**Collocation:** יַאְַזַנְיָהוּ

Jaazaniah \= man; ◄ son of Shaphan; ► idolatrous leader of Jerusalem in time of Ezekiel

[EZK 8:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:11)

Names of People

**Collocation:** יְזַנְיָה

Jezaniah \= man; ◄ son of Hoshaiah; ► Judean leader at time of exile

[JER 42:1](https://ref.ly/Jer42:1)1

1 JER 42:1 · JER 43:2 MT HOTTP\-B NIV NJPS CEV \| RSV NJB NRSV REB GNT עזריה cf.

Timothy L. Wilt

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 35:3; Ezekiel 11:1; 2 Kings 25:23; Jeremiah 40:8; Ezekiel 8:11; Jeremiah 42:1


## יָאִיר (id: 002739000000000)

יָאִיר
======

יָאִיר (H2971)

name · יָאִרִי

Names of People

Jair \= man; ◄ son of Segub, descendant of Manasseh; ► leader of conquest of a large area in Gilead during the settlement of Israel

[NUM 32:41](https://ref.ly/Num32:41) · [DEU 3:14](https://ref.ly/Deut3:14) · [1CH 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:22)

Names of People

Jair \= man; ◄ from Gilead; ► judge of Israel

[JDG 10:3](https://ref.ly/Judg10:3) · [JDG 10:5](https://ref.ly/Judg10:5)

Names of Locations

**Collocation:** חַוֹּת יָאִיר

Villages of Jair, Jair's Camps, Jair's Settlements \= settlements in Gilead, in certain passages associated with a person of the same name

[NUM 32:41](https://ref.ly/Num32:41) · [DEU 3:14](https://ref.ly/Deut3:14) · [JOS 13:30](https://ref.ly/Josh13:30) · [JDG 10:4](https://ref.ly/Judg10:4) · [1KI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:13) · [1CH 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:23)

Names of People

Jair \= man; ◄ tribe of Benjamin; ► father of Mordecai

[EST 2:5](https://ref.ly/Esth2:5)

Timothy L. Wilt

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 32:41; Deuteronomy 3:14; 1 Chronicles 2:22; Judges 10:3; Judges 10:5; Joshua 13:30; Judges 10:4; 1 Kings 4:13; 1 Chronicles 2:23; Esther 2:5


## יאל (id: 002740000000000)

יאל
===

יאל (H2973 · H2974)

verb · אִוֶּלֶת

Foolish

to be foolish, to be ignorant, to be without understanding \= to be and/or behave like one with little insight, education or wisdom; ≈ pejorative (see the related term אִוֶּלֶת)

[NUM 12:11](https://ref.ly/Num12:11) · [ISA 19:13](https://ref.ly/Isa19:13) · [JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4) · [JER 50:36](https://ref.ly/Jer50:36)

verb

Willing

to be willing, to accept (to do something) \= state in which humans and deities are willing to do something; often used in polite speech to soften one's request or to underline its sincerity

[EXO 2:21](https://ref.ly/Exod2:21) · [JOS 7:7](https://ref.ly/Josh7:7) · [JDG 17:11](https://ref.ly/Judg17:11) · [JDG 19:6](https://ref.ly/Judg19:6) · [1SA 12:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:22) · [2SA 7:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:29) · [2KI 5:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:23) · [2KI 6:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:3) · [1CH 17:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:27) · [JOB 6:9](https://ref.ly/Job6:9) · [JOB 6:28](https://ref.ly/Job6:28)

Think

to resolve, to persist \= action by which humans a take firm decision to do something, often in spite of the circumstances

[GEN 18:27](https://ref.ly/Gen18:27) · [GEN 18:31](https://ref.ly/Gen18:31) · [DEU 1:5](https://ref.ly/Deut1:5) · [JOS 17:12](https://ref.ly/Josh17:12) · [JDG 1:27](https://ref.ly/Judg1:27) · [JDG 1:35](https://ref.ly/Judg1:35) · [HOS 5:11](https://ref.ly/Hos5:11)

verb

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 12:11; Isaiah 19:13; Jeremiah 5:4; Jeremiah 50:36; Exodus 2:21; Joshua 7:7; Judges 17:11; Judges 19:6; 1 Samuel 12:22; 2 Samuel 7:29; 2 Kings 5:23; 2 Kings 6:3; 1 Chronicles 17:27; Job 6:9; Job 6:28; Genesis 18:27; Genesis 18:31; Deuteronomy 1:5; Joshua 17:12; Judges 1:27; Judges 1:35; Hosea 5:11


## יְאֹר (id: 002741000000000)

יְאֹר
=====

יְאֹר (H2975) — יְאֹור

noun m, name

Names of Waterbodies

river Nile \= a river ◄ flowing through Egypt from south to north; ► overflowing its banks each year and providing fertility for the fields on both sides; ≈ generally associated with Egypt's power and prosperity

[GEN 41:1](https://ref.ly/Gen41:1) · [GEN 41:2](https://ref.ly/Gen41:2) · [GEN 41:3](https://ref.ly/Gen41:3) · [GEN 41:3](https://ref.ly/Gen41:3) · [GEN 41:17](https://ref.ly/Gen41:17) · [GEN 41:18](https://ref.ly/Gen41:18) · [EXO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:22) · [EXO 2:3](https://ref.ly/Exod2:3) · [EXO 2:5](https://ref.ly/Exod2:5) · [EXO 2:5](https://ref.ly/Exod2:5) · [EXO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:9) · [EXO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:9) · [EXO 7:15](https://ref.ly/Exod7:15) · [EXO 7:17](https://ref.ly/Exod7:17) · [EXO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Exod7:18) · [EXO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Exod7:18) · [EXO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Exod7:18) · [EXO 7:20](https://ref.ly/Exod7:20) · [EXO 7:20](https://ref.ly/Exod7:20) · [EXO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:21) · [EXO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:21) · [EXO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:21) · [EXO 7:24](https://ref.ly/Exod7:24) · [EXO 7:24](https://ref.ly/Exod7:24) · [EXO 7:25](https://ref.ly/Exod7:25) · [EXO 7:28](https://ref.ly/Exod7:28) · [EXO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Exod8:5) · [EXO 8:7](https://ref.ly/Exod8:7) · [EXO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Exod17:5) · [ISA 19:7](https://ref.ly/Isa19:7) · [ISA 19:7](https://ref.ly/Isa19:7) · [ISA 19:7](https://ref.ly/Isa19:7) · [ISA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:8) · [ISA 23:3](https://ref.ly/Isa23:3) · [ISA 23:10](https://ref.ly/Isa23:10) · [JER 46:7](https://ref.ly/Jer46:7) · [JER 46:8](https://ref.ly/Jer46:8) · [EZK 29:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:9) · [AMO 8:8](https://ref.ly/Amos8:8) · [AMO 8:8](https://ref.ly/Amos8:8) · [AMO 9:5](https://ref.ly/Amos9:5) · [AMO 9:5](https://ref.ly/Amos9:5) · [ZEC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Zech10:11)

Names of Waterbodies

the river literally: "the river"; hence, probably: \= a reference to the Tigris or to the Euphrates

[DAN 12:5](https://ref.ly/Dan12:5) · [DAN 12:5](https://ref.ly/Dan12:5) · [DAN 12:6](https://ref.ly/Dan12:6) · [DAN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Dan12:7)

Waterbodies

branch of the Nile \= a body of water flowing to and from the Nile river, whether in the form of a manmade canal or a subsidiary river branch, especially those of the Nile delta region

[EXO 7:19](https://ref.ly/Exod7:19) · [EXO 8:1](https://ref.ly/Exod8:1) · [2KI 19:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:24) · [PSA 78:44](https://ref.ly/Ps78:44) · [ISA 7:18](https://ref.ly/Isa7:18) · [ISA 19:6](https://ref.ly/Isa19:6) · [ISA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Isa37:25) · [EZK 29:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:3) · [EZK 29:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:3) · [EZK 29:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:4) · [EZK 29:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:4) · [EZK 29:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:4) · [EZK 29:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:5) · [EZK 29:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:10) · [EZK 30:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:12) · [NAM 3:8](https://ref.ly/Nah3:8)

Waterbodies

stream \= a stream of flowing water in a general sense; ≈ symbolizing an abundance of water and the fertility it brings, resulting in further blessings

[ISA 33:21](https://ref.ly/Isa33:21)

Constructions

tunnel \= an underground passageway, ◄ dug by humans ► for mining purposes

[JOB 28:10](https://ref.ly/Job28:10)

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

## יאשׁ (id: 002743000000000)

יאשׁ
====

יאשׁ (H2976)

verb · יֹאשִׁיָּה

Willing

to give up, to consider useless, to consider hopeless \= process by which someone resigns to being unable to change a situation, and gives up trying to do so

[1SA 27:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:1) · [JOB 6:26](https://ref.ly/Job6:26) · [ISA 57:10](https://ref.ly/Isa57:10) · [JER 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jer2:25) · [JER 18:12](https://ref.ly/Jer18:12)

Willing

to cause to despair, to cause to give in \= process by which someone causes someone to accept the impossibility to change a situation

[ECC 2:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:20)

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 27:1; Job 6:26; Isaiah 57:10; Jeremiah 2:25; Jeremiah 18:12; Ecclesiastes 2:20

## יבב (id: 002747000000000)

יבב
===

יבב (H2980)

verb

See

to gaze, to peer \= action by which humans look intently in a certain direction

[JDG 5:28](https://ref.ly/Judg5:28)1

verb

Speak

to lament, to cry out \= action by which someone utters a cry, ► expressing feelings of concern

[JDG 5:28](https://ref.ly/Judg5:28)1

1 JDG 5:28 RSV NJB NRSV REB GNT CEV \[1] \| NIV NJPS \[2] \| according HOTTP\-A both meanings are possible \| some suggest נבט instead of יבב but according to HOTTP such an emendation is unnecessary

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 5:28

יְבוּסִי
========

יְבוּסִי (H2983)

adj · יְבוּס

noun f

Control

hand, charge, authority, control, power literally: hand; hence: \= state of being in a position of control and authority over objects, people or activities

[EZR 5:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:8) · [EZR 5:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:12) · [DAN 2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:38) · [DAN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:15) · [DAN 3:17](https://ref.ly/Dan3:17) · [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23) · [DAN 6:28](https://ref.ly/Dan6:28) · [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25)

Know

expertise literally: (having something in one's) hand; hence: \= state in which humans are an expert in a certain matter, ◄ as if having it totally mastered

[EZR 7:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:14)

Engage

to have (a certain quality) literally: (to be in one's) hands; hence: \= state in which humans possess a certain quality and have it influence their activities

[EZR 7:25](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:25)

Engage

**Collocation:** שׁלח יָד

to stretch out (one's) hand \> to lift a hand (to do something) literally: to stretch out (one's) hand (to do something); hence: \= action by which humans take a conscious decision to perform a certain event

[EZR 6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:12)

Non\-Engage

**Collocation:** מחא יָד

to stay (one's) hand, to stop literally: to strike (someone's) hand; hence: \= action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to stop engaging in a certain activity

[DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32)

Happen

hand (as metonym), person literally: hand, used metonymically to denote the owner of the hand; hence: \= the person who is the originator of a particular event

[DAN 2:34](https://ref.ly/Dan2:34) · [DAN 2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:45)

4:10](https://ref.ly/Zech4:10) · [ZEC 8:4](https://ref.ly/Zech8:4) · [ZEC 14:13](https://ref.ly/Zech14:13) · [ZEC 14:13](https://ref.ly/Zech14:13) · [ZEC 14:13](https://ref.ly/Zech14:13)

People

**Collocation:** בֵּין יָדַיִם

body, back, chest literally: between (one's) hands; hence: \= part of the human body, especially from the shoulders to the bottom, either front or back

[ZEC 13:6](https://ref.ly/Zech13:6)

Afraid

**Collocation:** רְפֵה יָדַיִם · רִפְיֹון יָדַיִם

discouraged literally: weak of hands; hence: \= state in which humans lack the confidence to perform a certain event

[2SA 17:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:2) · [JER 47:3](https://ref.ly/Jer47:3)

Afraid

**Collocation:** יָדַיִם רפה

to be discouraged, to lose heart literally: (one's) hands are weak; hence: \= state in which humans lack the confidence to perform a certain event

[2SA 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:1) · [2CH 15:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:7) · [NEH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:9) · [ISA 13:7](https://ref.ly/Isa13:7) · [JER 6:24](https://ref.ly/Jer6:24) · [JER 50:43](https://ref.ly/Jer50:43) · [EZK 7:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:17) · [EZK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:12) · [ZEP 3:16](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:16)

Afraid

**Collocation:** רפה יָדַיִם

to discourage literally: to make (someone's) hands weak; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to lack the courage to perform a certain event

[EZR 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:4) · [JER 38:4](https://ref.ly/Jer38:4) · [JER 38:4](https://ref.ly/Jer38:4)

Artifacts

tenon, axletree, handle literally: hand; hence: \= part of an object extending out in a certain direction, ◄ similar to function of the body's hand or arm

[EXO 26:17](https://ref.ly/Exod26:17) · [EXO 26:19](https://ref.ly/Exod26:19) · [EXO 26:19](https://ref.ly/Exod26:19) · [EXO 36:22](https://ref.ly/Exod36:22) · [EXO 36:24](https://ref.ly/Exod36:24) · [EXO 36:24](https://ref.ly/Exod36:24) · [1KI 7:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:32) · [1KI 7:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:33) · [1KI 7:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:35) · [1KI 7:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:36) · [1KI 10:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:19) · [1KI 10:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:19) · [2CH 9:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:18) · [2CH 9:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:18) · [ISA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9)

Associate

**Collocation:** משׁך יָד

to join hands with mockers literally: to drag out (one's) hand (with someone); hence, possibly: \= action by which humans associate with other humans, ◄ described with the image of someone stretching out one's hand to someone else asking that person to join in

[HOS 7:5](https://ref.ly/Hos7:5)

Associate

**Collocation:** שׁית יָד

to join hands (with someone) literally: to put (one's) hand with (someone else); hence: \= action by which humans associate with other humans

[EXO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1)

Bear

**Collocation:** תקע יָד

to strike hands (in pledge) \= causative action by which someone strikes the palm of their hand against someone else's palm; ► in order to show that one has taken responsibility for someone else's debts; ≈ usually regarded as unwise

[JOB 17:3](https://ref.ly/Job17:3)

Confident

**Collocation:** יָדַיִם חזק

to have courage \= literally: (one's) hands are strong; hence: \= state in which humans have the confidence to perform a certain event

[JDG 7:11](https://ref.ly/Judg7:11) · [2SA 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:7) · [2SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21) · [EZK 22:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:14) · [ZEC 8:9](https://ref.ly/Zech8:9) · [ZEC 8:13](https://ref.ly/Zech8:13)

Confident

**Collocation:** חזק יָד

to encourage (someone) literally: to strengthen (someone's) hands; hence: \= causative action by which human help other humans to find the courage to perform a certain event

[JDG 9:24](https://ref.ly/Judg9:24) · [1SA 23:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:16) · [EZR 6:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:22) · [NEH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Neh2:18) · [NEH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:9) · [JOB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Job4:3) · [ISA 35:3](https://ref.ly/Isa35:3) · [JER 23:14](https://ref.ly/Jer23:14) · [EZK 13:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:22) · [EZK 16:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:49)

Control

**Collocation:** שׂים יָדַיִם

to lay hands (on someone), to arrest literally: to lay hands (on someone); hence: \= action by which humans seize other humans and take control of them

[2KI 11:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:16) · [2CH 23:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:15)

Engage

**Collocation:** נשׂא יָד

to lift up (one's) hand \> to act literally: to lift up (one's) hand); hence: \= action by which deities get involved in an event after a time of inaction

[PSA 10:12](https://ref.ly/Ps10:12)

Non\-Engage

**Collocation:** רפה יָד

to stay (one's) hand, to stop literally: to weaken (one's) hand; hence: \= action by which humans or supernatural beings stop engaging in a certain activity

[JOS 10:6](https://ref.ly/Josh10:6) · [2SA 24:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:16) · [1CH 21:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:15)

Non\-Engage

**Collocation:** שׁוב יָד מִן

to withhold (one's) hand from literally: to turn (one's) hand from (an event); hence: \= action by which humans and deities purposefully refrain from engaing in a particular event

[LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8) · [EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8) · [EZK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:17) · [EZK 20:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:22)

Happen

hand (as metonym), person literally: hand, used metonymically to denote the owner of the hand; hence: \= the person who is the originator of a particular event

Happen

**Collocation:** בְּיָד

because of literally: by the hand (of someone); hence, possibly: \= state in which something is done because of another matter relating to a particular person

[JER 41:9](https://ref.ly/Jer41:9)1

Happen

**Collocation:** אנה לְיָד

to allow to happen literally: to cause to happen to (someone's) hand; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause or allow something to happen to somebody

[EXO 21:13](https://ref.ly/Exod21:13)

Safe

**Collocation:** חזק בְּיָד

to help, to support literally: to grasp (someone) by the hand; hence: \= action by which humans or deities offer assistance to (other) humans

Safe

**Collocation:** יָד אֵת · יָד עִם

to help, to support literally: (one's) hand is with (someone else); hence: \= action by which humans or deities offer assistance to (other) humans

Control

**Collocation:** עַל יָד · עַל יָדַיִם

under the direction of literally: according to the hand(s) (of someone); hence: \= state in which something takes place under the authority of someone else

[1CH 25:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:2) · [1CH 25:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:2) · [1CH 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:3) · [1CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6) · [1CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6) · [1CH 26:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:28) · [1CH 29:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:8) · [2CH 23:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:18) · [2CH 26:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:11) · [2CH 26:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:13) · [2CH 29:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:27) · [2CH 31:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:15) · [EZR 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:10) · [JER 5:31](https://ref.ly/Jer5:31)

Control

**Collocation:** עמד עַל יָדַיִם

to put (someone) in charge of (something) literally: to appoint (someone) over the hands (of an activity); hence: \= state in which someone has the authority over a certain activity

[1CH 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:16)

Imminent

**Collocation:** בְּיָד

imminent literally: in (one's) hand; hence: \= state in which an event is about to take place

[JOB 15:23](https://ref.ly/Job15:23)

Possess

in possession literally: (someone's) hand; hence: \= state in which humans own a particular object

Possess

**Collocation:** דבק בְּיָד

to stick to (one's) hand literally: to cleave to (one's) hand; hence: \= action by which humans take possession of an object that they carry or come across, without having the authority to do so

Bear

hand, responsibility literally: (someone's) hand; \= state in which humans bear the responsibility for a certain situation

Poor

**Collocation:** יָד מוט

to become dependent literally: (one's) hand wavers; hence: \= state in which humans are unable to provide for themselves

[LEV 25:35](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35)

Unwilling

**Collocation:** קפץ יָד

to be tight\-fisted literally: to shut (one's) hand; hence: \= state in which humans are unwilling to help out other humans with money or goods

[DEU 15:7](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7)

Willing

**Collocation:** פתח יָד

to give generously literally: to open (one's) hand; hence: \= state in which humans are very willing to help out other humans with money or goods

[DEU 15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:8) · [DEU 15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:11) · [PSA 104:28](https://ref.ly/Ps104:28)

Impact

hand, impactful involvement literally: hand; hence: \= action by which humans or deities engage in an act of violence against (other) humans

to eat literally: to bring (one's) hand to (one's) mouth; hence: \= action by which humans eat something

[1SA 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:26)

Judge

**Collocation:** שׁית יָד

to arbitrate literally: to lay (one's) hand on (two parties); hence: \= causative action by which humans resolve a conflict between two parties

Large

**Collocation:** רַחֲבַת יָדַיִם · רַחֲבֵי יָדַיִם

broad, spacious literally: wide of hands; hence: \= state in which a certain object has a relatively large distance from side to side

Lazy

**Collocation:** שִׁפְלוּת יָדַיִם

laziness, indolence literally: idleness of hands; hence: \= state in which humans avoid activity

[ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18)

Monuments

**Collocation:** יָד

monument, memorial literally: hand; hence: \= a pillar\-like object made out of rock, possibly with a hand engraved upon it (as has been found on other ancient monuments, as a symbol of power or of prayer) ► set up in honor or memorial of someone (known ancient ones are in honor of a king or god)

Spatial Referents

side, vicinity literally: hand; hence: \= a referent pointing to the location to the left or right of an object ◄ analogous to the location of someone's hands or arms

help, service literally: to the side (of someone or something); hence: \= state in which humans are available to provide help and assistance for the benefit of a person or place

Quantity

shares, portions, times, groups, divisions literally: hands; hence: \= more or less equal portions

Quantity

**Collocation:** עַל־יָד

little by little literally: by hand; hence: \= process by which a quantity increases in small increments

Role

**Collocation:** מלא יָד

to ordain, to install, to consecrate literally: to fill (someone's) hand; hence: \= causative action by which humans ordain other humans to the office of priest or a similar role

Holy

**Collocation:** מלא יָדִַים

Serve

**Collocation:** יצק מַיִם עַל יָדַיִם

Space

area, position literally: hand; hence: \= a space designated for a certain purpose

Non\-Speak

**Collocation:** שׂים יָד עַל־פֶּה

to keep (one's) mouth shut literally: to lay (one's) hand on (one's) mouth; hence: \= action by which humans purposefully refrain from speaking up

Stance

Confident

**Collocation:** נטה יָד

to stretch out (one's) hand, to defy literally: to stretch out (one's) hand (against someone); hence: \= action by which humans openly show resistance to someone else

Confident

**Collocation:** יָד רָמָה

boldly, defiantly literally: (to do something with) raised hand; hence: \= state in which humans or deities perform an action with confidence

Engage

**Collocation:** רום יָד

to lift a hand (to do something) literally: to raise (one's) hand (to do something); hence: \= action by which humans take a conscious decision to perform a certain event

Engage

**Collocation:** שׁלח יָד

to stretch out (one's) hand \> to lift a hand (to do something) literally: to stretch out (one's) hand (to do something); hence: \= action by which humans take a conscious decision to perform a certain event

Impact

**Collocation:** נוף יָד

Impact

**Collocation:** תְּנוּפַת יָד

raising (one's) hands (against someone) literally: waving of (one's) hand (against someone); hence: \= action by which deities engage in an act of violence against humans

[ISA 19:16](https://ref.ly/Isa19:16)

Impact

**Collocation:** נשׂא יָד

to lift up (one's) hand (against someone else) literally: to lift up (one's) hands (against someone); hence: \= action by which humans engage in an act of violence against other humans

Impact

**Collocation:** רום יָד

[1KI 11:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:26) · [1KI 11:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:27)

Impact

**Collocation:** שׁוב יָד

to strike literally: to turn (one's) hand against (someone else); hence: \= action by which humans or deities engage in an act of violence against (other) humans

Impact

**Collocation:** שׁלח יָד

Insignificant

Possess

**Collocation:** יָד נשׂג

to afford literally: (one's) hand catches up (with something); hence: \= state in which someone has sufficient resources to carry out a certain task

[LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11) · [LEV 14:21](https://ref.ly/Lev14:21) · [LEV 14:22](https://ref.ly/Lev14:22) · [LEV 14:30](https://ref.ly/Lev14:30) · [LEV 14:31](https://ref.ly/Lev14:31) · [LEV 14:32](https://ref.ly/Lev14:32) · [LEV 25:26](https://ref.ly/Lev25:26) · [LEV 25:47](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47) · [LEV 25:49](https://ref.ly/Lev25:49) · [LEV 27:8](https://ref.ly/Lev27:8) · [NUM 6:21](https://ref.ly/Num6:21) · [EZK 46:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:7)

Objects

**Collocation:** תְּשׂוּמֶת יָד

pledge \= object given in pledge

[LEV 5:21](https://ref.ly/Lev5:21)

Vow

**Collocation:** נשׂא יָד

to swear, to take an oath literally: to lift up (one's) hands; hence: \= action by which deities make a solemn promise, invoking themselves as a witness by lack of a higher power, which, in the case of humans, is usually done while raising one's hand

[EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8) · [NUM 14:30](https://ref.ly/Num14:30) · [DEU 32:40](https://ref.ly/Deut32:40) · [NEH 9:15](https://ref.ly/Neh9:15) · [PSA 106:26](https://ref.ly/Ps106:26) · [EZK 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:5) · [EZK 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:5) · [EZK 20:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:6) · [EZK 20:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:15) · [EZK 20:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:23) · [EZK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:28) · [EZK 20:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:42) · [EZK 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:7) · [EZK 44:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:12) · [EZK 47:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:14)

Vow

**Collocation:** רום יָד

to swear, to take an oath literally: to lift up (one's) hands; hence: \= action by which humans make a solemn promise, often invoking a higher power as a witness, which is usually done while raising one's hand

[GEN 14:22](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22)

Strong

hand, arm, strength literally: hand; hence: \= action by which humans or deities exert strength and power

Weak

**Collocation:** קִצְרֵי־יָד

powerless literally: short (of hand); hence: \= state in which humans lack the power to perform a certain event

[2KI 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:26) · [ISA 37:27](https://ref.ly/Isa37:27)

Tools

tool, implement literally: hand; hence: \= a handheld tool or implement of wood or stone

[NUM 35:17](https://ref.ly/Num35:17) · [NUM 35:18](https://ref.ly/Num35:18)

True

**Collocation:** יָד לְיָד

assuredly literally: hand to hand; hence: \= state in which the truth of a statement is confirmed

[PRO 11:21](https://ref.ly/Prov11:21) · [PRO 11:21](https://ref.ly/Prov11:21) · [PRO 16:5](https://ref.ly/Prov16:5) · [PRO 16:5](https://ref.ly/Prov16:5)

Turn

**Collocation:** הפך יָד

to turn (a chariot) literally: to turn (one's) hand(s); hence: \= causative action by which a charioteer causes his vehicle to turn around giving a firm pull on the reins of the horse

[1KI 22:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34) · [2KI 9:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:23) · [2CH 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33)

Vow

**Collocation:** נתן יָד

to pledge literally: to give (one's) hand (in pledge); hence: \= action by which humans make a solemn promise to do something

[EZR 10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:19) · [EZK 17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:18)

Weapons

**Collocation:** מַקֵּל יָד

war club, mace exact meaning unknown; possibly: \= a short wooden rod used for striking the enemy in hand\-to\-hand combat; it could be held with one hand and was used to strike the enemy on all parts of his body, particularly where he was unprotected by armor; possibly fitted with a heavy head made of stone or metal

[EZK 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:9)5

Willing

**Collocation:** כְּיַד מֶלֶךְ

bounty literally: as the hand of a king; hence: \= a state in which something is done without holding back as can be expected from a king

[1KI 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:13) · [EST 1:7](https://ref.ly/Esth1:7) · [EST 2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:18)

## ידד (id: 002794000000000)

ידד
===

ידד (H3032)

verb · יְדִדוּת · יָדִיד · יְדִידֹות · אֱלִידָד · יְדִידָה · יְדִידְיָהּ · אֶלְדָּד

Move

## ידה (id: 002796000000000)

ידה
===

ידה (A3029 · H3034) — ידא

verb · הֹוד · הֻיְּדֹות · תֹּודָה

Speak

**Collocation:** ידה שֵׁם

to confess (God's) name literally: to confess (God's) name; hence: \= action by which humans publicly admit their own shortcomings before God, or the shortcomings of the people they represent

[1KI 8:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:33) · [1KI 8:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:35) · [2CH 6:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:24) · [2CH 6:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:26) · [PSA 44:9](https://ref.ly/Ps44:9) · [PSA 54:8](https://ref.ly/Ps54:8) · [PSA 99:3](https://ref.ly/Ps99:3) · [PSA 138:2](https://ref.ly/Ps138:2) · [PSA 142:8](https://ref.ly/Ps142:8) · [ISA 25:1](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1)

Speak

to confess, to make confession \= action by which humans publicly admit their own shortcomings before God, or the shortcomings of the people they represent

6:11](https://ref.ly/Dan6:11)

verb

Move

to throw, to shoot \= action by which an object, used as a weapon, is caused to move forcefully in a certain direction

[JER 50:14](https://ref.ly/Jer50:14) · [LAM 3:53](https://ref.ly/Lam3:53)3

Move

to throw down \= action or process by which something is caused to move forcefully to the ground, ≈ an image of loss of power

[ZEC 2:4](https://ref.ly/Zech2:4)

1 2CH 30:22 NJPS "confessing" REB "making confession" \= \[1a] \| RSV NRSV ESV "giving thanks" NIV NJB CEV "praised" GNT "in praise" \= \[1b] 2 NEH 11:17 The expression יְהֹודֶה לַתְּפִלָּה may mean "to lead the prayer" as רֹאשׁ הַתְּחִלָּה "leader of the praise" 3 LAM 3:53 the singular אֶבֶן "stone", along with LXX's use of ἐπιτιθέναι to translate the verb leads many (cf. REB NJB) to interpret this verse in the sense indicated by GNT "to close (the opening of a pit) with a stone"

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

to have knowledge, to be experienced literally: to know; hence: \= state in which humans are endowed with knowledge, often through experience

## יָדָע (id: 002811000000000)

יָדָע
=====

יָדָע (H3047)

name

Names of People

Jada \= man; ◄ descendant of Jerahmeel; tribe of Judah

[1CH 2:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:28) · [1CH 2:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:32)

Timothy L. Wilt

## יְדַעְיָה (id: 002812000000000)

יְדַעְיָה
=========

יְדַעְיָה (H3048)

name

Names of People

Jedaiah \= man; ◄ tribe of Levi; ► head of family of priests returned from exile

[1CH 9:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:10) · [EZR 2:36](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:36) · [NEH 7:39](https://ref.ly/Neh7:39) · [NEH 11:10](https://ref.ly/Neh11:10) · [NEH 12:6](https://ref.ly/Neh12:6)

Names of People

Jedaiah \= man; ◄ tribe of Levi; ► family head in charge of a division of priests; time of David

[1CH 24:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:7)

Names of People

Jedaiah \= various men; ◄ tribe of Levi; ► priests in Judah, after exile

[NEH 12:7](https://ref.ly/Neh12:7) · [NEH 12:19](https://ref.ly/Neh12:19) · [NEH 12:21](https://ref.ly/Neh12:21)

Names of People

Jedaiah \= man; ► returned from exile

[ZEC 6:10](https://ref.ly/Zech6:10) · [ZEC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Zech6:14)

Timothy L. Wilt

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 9:10; Ezra 2:36; Nehemiah 7:39; Nehemiah 11:10; Nehemiah 12:6; 1 Chronicles 24:7; Nehemiah 12:7; Nehemiah 12:19; Nehemiah 12:21; Zechariah 6:10; Zechariah 6:14


## יִדְּעֹנִי (id: 002813000000000)

יִדְּעֹנִי
==========

יִדְּעֹנִי (H3049)

noun m · ידע

spirit of the dead \= spirit of a dead person, ► consulted by fortune\-tellers; ≈ an activity forbidden in the law of Moses

[LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31) · [LEV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Lev20:6) · [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27) · [DEU 18:11](https://ref.ly/Deut18:11) · [2KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:6) · [2CH 33:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:6) · [ISA 8:19](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19) · [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3)

Professions

one who consults the spirits of the dead \= person who is said to be able to communicate with spirits of the deceased

[1SA 28:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:3) · [1SA 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:9) · [2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24)

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:31; Leviticus 20:6; Leviticus 20:27; Deuteronomy 18:11; 2 Kings 21:6; 2 Chronicles 33:6; Isaiah 8:19; Isaiah 19:3; 1 Samuel 28:3; 1 Samuel 28:9; 2 Kings 23:24


## יָהּ (id: 002814000000000)

יָהּ
====

יָהּ (H3050)

name

Names of Deities

Yah, YHWH \= short form of יהוה, only found in poetic texts

## יהב (id: 002815000000000)

יהב
===

יהב (H3051 · A3052)

verb

Non\-Possess

to give \= causative action by which humans transfer the possession of an object to someone else

[GEN 47:15](https://ref.ly/Gen47:15) · [GEN 47:16](https://ref.ly/Gen47:16) · [JDG 1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:15) · [EZR 4:20](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:20) · [EZR 6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:4) · [EZR 6:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:8) · [EZR 6:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:9) · [JOB 6:22](https://ref.ly/Job6:22) · [PRO 30:15](https://ref.ly/Prov30:15) · [PRO 30:15](https://ref.ly/Prov30:15) · [DAN 2:48](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48) · [DAN 5:17](https://ref.ly/Dan5:17) · [ZEC 11:12](https://ref.ly/Zech11:12)

Birth

**Collocation:** יהב בָּנִים

to give (someone) children literally: to give (someone) children; hence: \= causative action by which humans enable their spouses to bring forth children

[GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1)

Control

**Collocation:** יהב בְּיָד

to hand over, to deliver in the hands (of someone else) literally: to give (someone) in the hands (of someone else); hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to be in a position of control or authority over others

[EZR 5:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:12) · [DAN 2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:38) · [DAN 7:11](https://ref.ly/Dan7:11) · [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25)

Control

**Collocation:** יהב טְעֵם

to give account, to be accountable literally: to give a report; hence: \= state in which humans are under the authority of other humans

[DAN 6:3](https://ref.ly/Dan6:3)

Dead

**Collocation:** יהב גְּשֵׁם

to give up (one's) body literally: to give (one's) body); hence: \= action by which humans allow themselves to be killed for a certain cause

[DAN 3:28](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28)

Engage

to grant help, to give aid, to help literally: to give; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to receive a quality, a position, or support

[JDG 20:7](https://ref.ly/Judg20:7) · [2SA 16:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:20) · [PSA 60:13](https://ref.ly/Ps60:13) · [PSA 108:13](https://ref.ly/Ps108:13) · [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21) · [DAN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Dan2:23) · [DAN 2:37](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37) · [DAN 4:13](https://ref.ly/Dan4:13) · [DAN 5:18](https://ref.ly/Dan5:18) · [DAN 5:19](https://ref.ly/Dan5:19) · [DAN 5:28](https://ref.ly/Dan5:28) · [DAN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Dan7:4) · [DAN 7:6](https://ref.ly/Dan7:6) · [DAN 7:12](https://ref.ly/Dan7:12) · [DAN 7:14](https://ref.ly/Dan7:14) · [DAN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:22) · [DAN 7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:27)

Evaluators

come on! hey! literally: give!; hence: \= exclamation encouraging people to act

[GEN 11:3](https://ref.ly/Gen11:3) · [GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4) · [GEN 11:7](https://ref.ly/Gen11:7) · [GEN 38:16](https://ref.ly/Gen38:16) · [EXO 1:10](https://ref.ly/Exod1:10)

Hold

to hold literally: to give; hence: \= causative action by which humans transfer an object to other humans on a temporary basis

[RUT 3:15](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:15) · [EZR 5:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:14) · [EZR 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:19)

Associate

to give (someone a spouse) literally: to give (someone a spouse); hence: \= causative action by which parents allow their child to become someone's spouse

[GEN 29:21](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21)

Know

to ascribe, to acknowledge literally: to give (a quality); hence: \= action by which humans publicly acknowledge one of the qualities of a deity

[DEU 32:3](https://ref.ly/Deut32:3) · [1CH 16:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:28) · [1CH 16:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:28) · [1CH 16:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:29) · [PSA 29:1](https://ref.ly/Ps29:1) · [PSA 29:1](https://ref.ly/Ps29:1) · [PSA 29:2](https://ref.ly/Ps29:2) · [PSA 96:7](https://ref.ly/Ps96:7) · [PSA 96:7](https://ref.ly/Ps96:7) · [PSA 96:8](https://ref.ly/Ps96:8)

Dissociate

to select, to provide literally: to give; hence: \= action by which humans or deities make a selection among several options, which include humans, divine beings, animals, other objects, or events

[DEU 1:13](https://ref.ly/Deut1:13) · [JOS 18:4](https://ref.ly/Josh18:4) · [1SA 14:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:41)

Space

to place literally: to give; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans or objects to be placed in a certain position

[2SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:15) · [EZR 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:16)

1 HOS 4:18 NIV ESV "dearly" NJPS "beyond measure"

Timothy L. Wilt; Enio R. Mueller

## יהד (id: 002817000000000)

יהד
===

יהד (H3054)

verb · יְהוּדִי

Associate

to become a Jew, to make oneself a Jew \= action or process by which someone presents oneself as a יְהוּדִי Jew, or makes oneself a Jew, ► which could suggest a temporary profession of convenience but more likely means adopting their customs, including, as LXX explicitly states, circumcision

## יְהוּדִי (id: 002824000000000)

יְהוּדִי
========

יְהוּדִי (H3057 · H3064 · H3065 · H3066)

adj · יְהוּדָה

Names of Languages

Judean, Hebrew \= language ◄ spoken by people of the kingdom of Judah

[2KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:26) · [2KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:28) · [2CH 32:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:18) · [NEH 13:24](https://ref.ly/Neh13:24) · [ISA 36:11](https://ref.ly/Isa36:11) · [ISA 36:13](https://ref.ly/Isa36:13)

Names of Groups

Judean, Jew \= member of tribe of Judah, inhabitant of the kingdom of Judah; after the exile referring to all descendants of Israel

## יָהִיר (id: 002845000000000)

יָהִיר
======

יָהִיר (H3093)

adj

Great

presumptuous, haughty \= pertaining to a failure to see the limits of what is permitted or appropriate in view of one's status, and act accordingly, especially with one's mouth; ≈ seen as a condition that cannot last

[PRO 21:24](https://ref.ly/Prov21:24) · [HAB 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hab2:5)

noun m · יֹומָם

Time

day \= the time of day that it is light, as opposed to the time that it is dark

14:7](https://ref.ly/Zech14:7)

Time

day, time, when \= a basic unit of time, including evening and morning; seven days make a week; sometimes used in a figurative sense referring to a limited period of time, which may be shorter or slightly longer than a single day; the context often makes it difficult to determine

days, time, period, season, life literally: days; hence: \= an unspecified amount of time

## יֹונָה (id: 002862000000000)

יֹונָה
======

יֹונָה (H3123 · H3124)

noun f

Birds

dove, pigeon \= a bird with a blue\-gray color with a pinkish sheen to the neck feathers and a black tip on its tail; lives in large colonies and eats mainly seeds; courts, mates, and has nests several times a year; nests on rock ledges; ◄ \|iColumba livia\|i\*; ► used as food and for sacrifices; ≈ regarded as swift, docile, and innocent, but also as silly and senseless; associated with affection, fertility and sexuality because of its mating habits; the moaning sound this bird sometimes makes is associated with pain and suffering

Great

## יחל (id: 002911000000000)

יחל
===

יחל (H2342 · H3176)

verb · יָחִיל · תֹּוחֶלֶת

Non\-Engage

to wait \= action by which humans delay action until the right moment, with a certain amount of confidence that this moment will actually come

[GEN 8:12](https://ref.ly/Gen8:12) · [1SA 10:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:8) · [1SA 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:8) · [2SA 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:14) · [JOB 14:14](https://ref.ly/Job14:14) · [JOB 29:21](https://ref.ly/Job29:21) · [JOB 29:23](https://ref.ly/Job29:23) · [JOB 32:11](https://ref.ly/Job32:11) · [JOB 32:16](https://ref.ly/Job32:16)

Confident

to hope for literally: to wait; hence: \= state in which humans are confident that their longing will be fulfilled

[2KI 6:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:33) · [JOB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Job13:15) · [JOB 30:26](https://ref.ly/Job30:26) · [PSA 31:25](https://ref.ly/Ps31:25) · [PSA 33:18](https://ref.ly/Ps33:18) · [PSA 33:22](https://ref.ly/Ps33:22) · [PSA 38:16](https://ref.ly/Ps38:16) · [PSA 42:6](https://ref.ly/Ps42:6) · [PSA 42:12](https://ref.ly/Ps42:12) · [PSA 43:5](https://ref.ly/Ps43:5) · [PSA 69:4](https://ref.ly/Ps69:4) · [PSA 71:14](https://ref.ly/Ps71:14) · [PSA 119:43](https://ref.ly/Ps119:43) · [PSA 119:49](https://ref.ly/Ps119:49) · [PSA 119:74](https://ref.ly/Ps119:74) · [PSA 119:81](https://ref.ly/Ps119:81) · [PSA 119:114](https://ref.ly/Ps119:114) · [PSA 119:147](https://ref.ly/Ps119:147) · [PSA 130:5](https://ref.ly/Ps130:5) · [PSA 130:7](https://ref.ly/Ps130:7) · [PSA 131:3](https://ref.ly/Ps131:3) · [PSA 147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:11) · [ISA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Isa42:4) · [ISA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Isa51:5) · [LAM 3:21](https://ref.ly/Lam3:21) · [LAM 3:24](https://ref.ly/Lam3:24) · [EZK 13:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:6) · [EZK 19:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:5) · [MIC 5:6](https://ref.ly/Mic5:6) · [MIC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Mic7:7)

## יטב (id: 002923000000000)

יטב
===

יטב (H3190 · A3191)

verb · מֵיטָב

Good

to tend, to adorn, to improve \= causative action by which humans cause objects to be in a better state of maintenance than before

[EXO 30:7](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7) · [2KI 9:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:30) · [HOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Hos10:1)

Great

**Collocation:** יטב שֵׁם

to make (someone) famous literally: to make (someone's) name good; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to have a good reputation

[1KI 1:47](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:47)

Intense

to enhance literally: to make good; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause a certain activity to take place with enhanced intensity and quality

[RUT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:10) · [PRO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Prov15:2) · [PRO 17:22](https://ref.ly/Prov17:22)

Rejoice

**Collocation:** לֵב יטב

to be happy literally: (one's) heart is good; hence: \= state in which humans feel happy and contented

[JDG 18:20](https://ref.ly/Judg18:20) · [JDG 19:6](https://ref.ly/Judg19:6) · [JDG 19:9](https://ref.ly/Judg19:9) · [RUT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:7) · [1KI 21:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:7) · [ECC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:3)

Rejoice

**Collocation:** לֵב יטב

to give joy literally: (one's) heart makes (someone) good; hence: \= causative process by which one's thinking and choices bring about enjoyment

[ECC 11:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:9)

Rejoice

**Collocation:** יטב לֵב

to enjoy (oneself) literally: to make (one's) heart good; hence: \= action by which humans act with happiness and contentment

[JDG 19:22](https://ref.ly/Judg19:22)

Right

to seem good (to someone), to please \= state in which humans or deities consider a matter or an activity morally and/or ethically right

Right

Right

to make right literally: to make right; hence: \= action by which humans perform their activities in a morally and/or ethically acceptable way

Safe

## יכח (id: 002929000000000)

יכח
===

יכח (H3198)

verb · תֹּוכֵחָה · תֹּוכַחַת

Judge

to settle disputes, arbiter \= action by which someone with judicial authority settles a dispute between two parties

[GEN 31:37](https://ref.ly/Gen31:37) · [JOB 9:33](https://ref.ly/Job9:33) · [JOB 16:21](https://ref.ly/Job16:21) · [ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4) · [ISA 11:3](https://ref.ly/Isa11:3) · [ISA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Isa11:4) · [ISA 29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:21) · [AMO 5:10](https://ref.ly/Amos5:10) · [MIC 4:3](https://ref.ly/Mic4:3)

Chastise

to correct, to punish literally: to judge; hence: \= action by which humans or deities inflict a penalty on others as retribution for what they have done

[2SA 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:14) · [2KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:4) · [1CH 12:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:18) · [1CH 16:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:21) · [JOB 5:17](https://ref.ly/Job5:17) · [JOB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Job13:10) · [JOB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Job13:10) · [JOB 33:19](https://ref.ly/Job33:19) · [PSA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2) · [PSA 38:2](https://ref.ly/Ps38:2) · [PSA 94:10](https://ref.ly/Ps94:10) · [PSA 141:5](https://ref.ly/Ps141:5) · [PRO 3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:12) · [PRO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Prov9:7) · [PRO 9:8](https://ref.ly/Prov9:8) · [PRO 9:8](https://ref.ly/Prov9:8) · [PRO 15:12](https://ref.ly/Prov15:12) · [PRO 19:25](https://ref.ly/Prov19:25) · [PRO 24:25](https://ref.ly/Prov24:25) · [ISA 37:4](https://ref.ly/Isa37:4) · [JER 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19) · [HAB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Hab1:12)

Innocent

to be vindicated literally: to be judged; hence: \= process by which humans are declared innocent by someone with judicial authority

[GEN 20:16](https://ref.ly/Gen20:16) · [JOB 23:7](https://ref.ly/Job23:7)

Rebuke

to rebuke, to refute, to argue, to warn \= action by which humans or deities express their disapproval of something with words

[GEN 21:25](https://ref.ly/Gen21:25) · [GEN 31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:42) · [LEV 19:17](https://ref.ly/Lev19:17) · [LEV 19:17](https://ref.ly/Lev19:17) · [JOB 6:25](https://ref.ly/Job6:25) · [JOB 6:25](https://ref.ly/Job6:25) · [JOB 6:26](https://ref.ly/Job6:26) · [JOB 13:3](https://ref.ly/Job13:3) · [JOB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Job13:15) · [JOB 15:3](https://ref.ly/Job15:3) · [JOB 19:5](https://ref.ly/Job19:5) · [JOB 22:4](https://ref.ly/Job22:4) · [JOB 32:12](https://ref.ly/Job32:12) · [JOB 40:2](https://ref.ly/Job40:2) · [PSA 50:8](https://ref.ly/Ps50:8) · [PSA 50:21](https://ref.ly/Ps50:21) · [PSA 105:14](https://ref.ly/Ps105:14) · [PRO 25:12](https://ref.ly/Prov25:12) · [PRO 28:23](https://ref.ly/Prov28:23) · [PRO 30:6](https://ref.ly/Prov30:6) · [EZK 3:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:26) · [HOS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Hos4:4) · [MIC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mic6:2)

Reconcile

to reason together \= action by which someone with judicial authority settles a dispute with the accused

[ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18)

Role

to appoint literally: to judge; hence: \= action by which someone with judicial authority singles out a person

[GEN 24:14](https://ref.ly/Gen24:14) · [GEN 24:44](https://ref.ly/Gen24:44)

## יָכִין (id: 002931000000000)

יָכִין
======

יָכִין (H3199)

name · כון · יָכִינִי

Names of People

Jachin \= man, also known as Jarib; ◄ son of Simeon

[GEN 46:10](https://ref.ly/Gen46:10) · [EXO 6:15](https://ref.ly/Exod6:15) · [NUM 26:12](https://ref.ly/Num26:12)

Names of People

Jachin \= man; ◄ tribe of Levi; ► priest; after exile

[1CH 9:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:10) · [NEH 11:10](https://ref.ly/Neh11:10)

Names of People

Jachin \= man; ◄ tribe of Levi; ► family head in charge of a division of priests; time of king David

[1CH 24:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:17)

Names of Constructions

Jachin \= pillar ◄ in temple of Solomon

[1KI 7:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:21) · [2CH 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:17)

Birth

to cause to bring \\birth \= causative process by which deities cause females among humans or animals to give birth to offspring through the process of conception, pregnancy, and labor

[ISA 66:9](https://ref.ly/Isa66:9) · [ISA 66:9](https://ref.ly/Isa66:9)

Birth

to act as midwife, to deliver \= action by which humans assist women during the process of childbirth

[EXO 1:16](https://ref.ly/Exod1:16)

Exist

to cause to bring forth literally: to cause to give birth; hence: \= causative process by which things are brough into existence

to bring to happen \= causative process by which something is caused to happen

Write

to register oneself \= action by which humans record their birth and descent

People

child \= a person in the beginning of life

## יֶלֶק (id: 002946000000000)

יֶלֶק
=====

יֶלֶק (H3218)

noun m

Swarming Creatures

locust term found primarily in poetic contexts: \= six\-legged, winged insect that is characterized by the fact that its third pair of legs is elongated and adapted to hopping; insects of this type move in swarms at certain periods and migrate to new areas, which they colonize; at other times they live either solitary or in small groups; ◄ Acrididae family; ► normally eaten cooked by toasting, grilling, frying, or broiling, though in some places they are also eaten raw

[PSA 105:34](https://ref.ly/Ps105:34) · [JER 51:14](https://ref.ly/Jer51:14) · [JER 51:27](https://ref.ly/Jer51:27) · [JOL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Joel1:4) · [JOL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Joel1:4) · [JOL 2:25](https://ref.ly/Joel2:25) · [NAM 3:15](https://ref.ly/Nah3:15) · [NAM 3:15](https://ref.ly/Nah3:15) · [NAM 3:16](https://ref.ly/Nah3:16)

Reinier de Blois

Names of Waterbodies

**Collocation:** יָם הַמֶּלַח

Salt Sea, Dead Sea literally: the sea of salt; hence: \= a large body of salt water located in the basin of the Jordan Valley, east of Judah; the Jordan River flows from the north into the northern part of this sea; the water is so salty that no living organism inhabits them

Names of Waterbodies

**Collocation:** יָם כִּנֶּרֶת

Sea of Chinnereth, Sea of Kinnereth, Lake Galilee \= a large body of fresh water east of the territory of Galilee, in northern Israel, through which the Jordan River flows

Names of Waterbodies

**Collocation:** יָם מִצְרַיִם

Sea of Egypt literally: sea of Egypt; hence, possibly: \= a reference to the Red Sea which is shaped like a tongue

[ISA 11:15](https://ref.ly/Isa11:15)

Names of Waterbodies

**Collocation:** יַם סוּף

Sea of Reeds, Gulf of Aqaba \= bodies of water located east of Egypt and south of Israel; possibly referring to the Gulf of Suez and/or to the Gulf of Aqaba off the Red Sea

sea, Persian Gulf, Euphrates River literally: the sea; hence: \= a body of water in the area of Babylon; possibly the Euphrates River or the Persian Gulf

## יָמִין (id: 002953000000000)

יָמִין
======

יָמִין (H3225 · H3226)

noun f · ימן

Spatial Referents

right side, right \= a referent pointing to the south side of the human body when facing the sunrise, ≈ and, since most people favor the hand on this side of the body, often associated with favor and preference

יְמִינִי
========

יְמִינִי (H3227a · H3227b)

adj · ימן

Spatial Referents

right \= a referent pointing to the south side of the human body when facing the sunrise, ≈ and, since most people favor the hand on this side of the body, often associated with favor and preference

name

Benjamin \= man, tribe, and territory; ◄ twelfth and last son of Jacob and second son of Rachel; ► founder of one of the tribes of Israel

Ingest

to suck \= action by which newborn children drink milk from their mother's breasts

[JOB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Job3:12) · [SNG 8:1](https://ref.ly/Song8:1) · [ISA 60:16](https://ref.ly/Isa60:16) · [ISA 60:16](https://ref.ly/Isa60:16) · [ISA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Isa66:11) · [ISA 66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12)

Ingest

to nurse \= causative action by which a human or animal mother feeds her newborn child from her breasts

[GEN 21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:7) · [GEN 32:16](https://ref.ly/Gen32:16) · [EXO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Exod2:7) · [EXO 2:9](https://ref.ly/Exod2:9) · [EXO 2:9](https://ref.ly/Exod2:9) · [1SA 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:23) · [1KI 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:21) · [LAM 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lam4:3)

Ingest

to suck literally: to suck; \= action by which humans ingest food

[JOB 20:16](https://ref.ly/Job20:16)

People

baby, infant \= a child that is still drinking milk from its mother's breasts

[NUM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:12) · [DEU 32:25](https://ref.ly/Deut32:25) · [1SA 15:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:3) · [1SA 22:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:19) · [PSA 8:3](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3) · [ISA 11:8](https://ref.ly/Isa11:8) · [JER 44:7](https://ref.ly/Jer44:7) · [LAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11) · [LAM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Lam4:4) · [JOL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Joel2:16)

Possess

**Collocation:** מֵינֶקֶת

to acquire literally: to suck; hence: \= action by which humans acquire possessions

[DEU 33:19](https://ref.ly/Deut33:19)

Possess

to provide literally: to nurse; hence: \= causative action by which deities provide humans with goods

[DEU 32:13](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13)

Professions

nurse \= a woman whose responsibility it is to breastfeed someone else's baby

[GEN 24:59](https://ref.ly/Gen24:59) · [GEN 35:8](https://ref.ly/Gen35:8) · [EXO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Exod2:7) · [2KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:2) · [2CH 22:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:11) · [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23)

## יַנְשֹׁוף (id: 002970000000000)

יַנְשֹׁוף
=========

יַנְשֹׁוף (H3244) — יַנְשׁוּף

noun m

Birds

tawny owl exact meaning unknown; possibly: type of owl with a mottled gray\-brown color; its eyes are outlined with pale circles, so that it looks as though it is wearing spectacles; it prefers wooded areas or orchards and roosts close to the trunk of a tree; ◄ Strix aluco; ► unclean and therefore not to be eaten

[LEV 11:17](https://ref.ly/Lev11:17)1 · [DEU 14:16](https://ref.ly/Deut14:16)2 · [ISA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Isa34:11)3

1 LEV 11:17 RSV "ibis", NIV NJPS NRSV "great owl" \| NJB "barn owl", REB "screech\-owl" 2 DEU 14:16 RSV NIV NJPS NRSV "great owl" \| NJB "barn owl" \| REB "screech\-owl" 3 ISA 34:11 RSV NRSV NJB "owl" \| NIV NJPS "great owl" \| REB "screech\-owl"

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 11:17; Deuteronomy 14:16; Isaiah 34:11


## יסד (id: 002971000000000)

יסד
===

יסד (H3245)

verb · יְסֻד · יְסֹוד · יְסוּדָה · מֹוסָד · מוּסָד · מֹוסָדָה · מוּסָדָה · מַסַּד

Space

to lay the foundations \= causative action by which humans put in place the bottom part of a building or larger structure, usually below the ground level, which will have to bear the entire construction

[JOS 6:26](https://ref.ly/Josh6:26) · [1KI 5:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:31) · [1KI 6:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:37) · [1KI 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:10) · [1KI 16:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:34) · [EZR 3:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:6) · [EZR 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:10) · [EZR 3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11) · [EZR 3:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:12) · [JOB 38:4](https://ref.ly/Job38:4) · [PSA 24:2](https://ref.ly/Ps24:2) · [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16) · [ISA 44:28](https://ref.ly/Isa44:28) · [ISA 54:11](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11) · [HAG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hag2:18) · [ZEC 4:9](https://ref.ly/Zech4:9) · [ZEC 8:9](https://ref.ly/Zech8:9)

Endure

to establish literally: to lay the foundation (for a decree); hence: \= action by which deities issue orders that is supposed to remain valid indefinitely

[PSA 119:152](https://ref.ly/Ps119:152)

Exist

to found, to build, to rebuild, to establish literally: to found; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities bring something into existence

[EXO 9:18](https://ref.ly/Exod9:18) · [2CH 24:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:27) · [2CH 31:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:7) · [PSA 78:69](https://ref.ly/Ps78:69) · [PSA 89:12](https://ref.ly/Ps89:12) · [PSA 102:26](https://ref.ly/Ps102:26) · [PSA 104:5](https://ref.ly/Ps104:5) · [PRO 3:19](https://ref.ly/Prov3:19) · [SNG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Song5:15) · [ISA 14:32](https://ref.ly/Isa14:32) · [ISA 48:13](https://ref.ly/Isa48:13) · [ISA 51:13](https://ref.ly/Isa51:13) · [ISA 51:16](https://ref.ly/Isa51:16) · [AMO 9:6](https://ref.ly/Amos9:6) · [ZEC 12:1](https://ref.ly/Zech12:1)

Role

to establish literally: to lay as a foundation; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities assign a role to (other) humans

[1CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:22) · [HAB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Hab1:12)

Solid

to be firm \= state in which an object can bear an exceedingly heavy weight, and so can be trusted to function as it is supposed to

[ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16)

Strong

**Collocation:** יסד עֹז

to establish strength literally: to lay the foundation of strength; hence: \= action by which deities draw strength out of a certain event

[PSA 8:3](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3)

Think

to give orders, to assign, to fix literally: to lay as foundation; hence: \= action by which humans or deities make a firm decision about something

[2CH 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:3) · [EST 1:8](https://ref.ly/Esth1:8) · [PSA 104:8](https://ref.ly/Ps104:8) · [ISA 23:13](https://ref.ly/Isa23:13)

verb

Think

to conspire, to scheme only found in poetry; \= action by which humans discuss a particular course of action ► with the purpose of doing something that will negatively affect others

[PSA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2) · [PSA 31:14](https://ref.ly/Ps31:14)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Joshua 6:26; 1 Kings 5:31; 1 Kings 6:37; 1 Kings 7:10; 1 Kings 16:34; Ezra 3:6; Ezra 3:10; Ezra 3:11; Ezra 3:12; Job 38:4; Psalms 24:2; Isaiah 28:16; Isaiah 44:28; Isaiah 54:11; Haggai 2:18; Zechariah 4:9; Zechariah 8:9; Psalms 119:152; Exodus 9:18; 2 Chronicles 24:27; 2 Chronicles 31:7; Psalms 78:69; Psalms 89:12; Psalms 102:26; Psalms 104:5; Proverbs 3:19; Song of Songs 5:15; Isaiah 14:32; Isaiah 48:13; Isaiah 51:13; Isaiah 51:16; Amos 9:6; Zechariah 12:1; 1 Chronicles 9:22; Habakkuk 1:12; Psalms 8:3; 2 Chronicles 3:3; Esther 1:8; Psalms 104:8; Isaiah 23:13; Psalms 2:2; Psalms 31:14

foundation, base \= the bottom part of a construction, which bears everything built on top of it

Foundation Gate \= one of the gates of the temple in Jerusalem

Engage

to add literally: to add (an activity to one's activities); hence: \= action by which humans add additional activities to those that they are currently engaged in

[DEU 4:2](https://ref.ly/Deut4:2) · [DEU 13:1](https://ref.ly/Deut13:1) · [1SA 12:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:19) · [JOB 34:37](https://ref.ly/Job34:37) · [PRO 30:6](https://ref.ly/Prov30:6) · [ISA 29:1](https://ref.ly/Isa29:1) · [JER 7:21](https://ref.ly/Jer7:21) · [JER 36:32](https://ref.ly/Jer36:32)

Engage

to add literally: to add (an activity to someone else's activities); hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities add additional activities to those that other humans or deities are currently engaged in

[1KI 12:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:11) · [1KI 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:14) · [2CH 10:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:11) · [2CH 10:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:14) · [2CH 28:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:13) · [EZR 10:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:10) · [NEH 13:18](https://ref.ly/Neh13:18) · [PSA 120:3](https://ref.ly/Ps120:3) · [PRO 3:2](https://ref.ly/Prov3:2) · [PRO 10:22](https://ref.ly/Prov10:22) · [ECC 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:14) · [JER 45:3](https://ref.ly/Jer45:3) · [EZK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:16)

Intense

to increase (in a quality), to grow literally: to add; hence: \= process by which a certain quality of humans increases in intensity, either in actual fact or in the eyes of others

[1KI 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:7) · [2CH 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:6) · [JOB 17:9](https://ref.ly/Job17:9) · [PRO 1:5](https://ref.ly/Prov1:5) · [PRO 9:9](https://ref.ly/Prov9:9) · [PRO 16:21](https://ref.ly/Prov16:21) · [PRO 16:23](https://ref.ly/Prov16:23) · [ECC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:16) · [ECC 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:18) · [ECC 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:18) · [DAN 4:33](https://ref.ly/Dan4:33)

Intense

to increase, to carry further literally: to add; hence: \= action by which humans increase the intensity of their engagement in a particular activity

[PSA 71:14](https://ref.ly/Ps71:14) · [EZK 23:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:14)

Respect

Old

years are added (to one's) life, to live a long time literally: years are added (to one's) life; hence: \= process by which humans are allowed to live longer than expected

Old

**Collocation:** יסף יָמִים · יסף שָׁנֹות

to add days or years to someone's life, to allow (someone) to live longer literally: to add days or years; hence: \= causative action by which deities allow humans to live longer than expected

## יסר (id: 002980000000000)

יסר
===

יסר (H3256)

verb · יִסֹּור · מוּסָר

Chastise

to discipline \= action by which humans or deities respond to negative behavior of (other) humans by administering some sort of punishment, which may range from a verbal rebuke to physical correction, ► often done with corrective intent, but sometimes also as retribution

[LEV 26:18](https://ref.ly/Lev26:18) · [LEV 26:28](https://ref.ly/Lev26:28) · [DEU 8:5](https://ref.ly/Deut8:5) · [DEU 8:5](https://ref.ly/Deut8:5) · [DEU 21:18](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18) · [DEU 22:18](https://ref.ly/Deut22:18) · [PSA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2) · [PSA 38:2](https://ref.ly/Ps38:2) · [PSA 39:12](https://ref.ly/Ps39:12) · [PSA 94:10](https://ref.ly/Ps94:10) · [PSA 118:18](https://ref.ly/Ps118:18) · [PSA 118:18](https://ref.ly/Ps118:18) · [PRO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Prov9:7) · [PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18) · [PRO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Prov29:17) · [PRO 29:19](https://ref.ly/Prov29:19) · [JER 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19) · [JER 10:24](https://ref.ly/Jer10:24) · [JER 30:11](https://ref.ly/Jer30:11) · [JER 31:18](https://ref.ly/Jer31:18) · [JER 46:28](https://ref.ly/Jer46:28) · [HOS 7:12](https://ref.ly/Hos7:12) · [HOS 10:10](https://ref.ly/Hos10:10)1

Willing

to accept discipline literally: to be disciplined; hence: \= action by which humans bring themselves to a position of submission before God, as a result of his disciplinary actions

[LEV 26:23](https://ref.ly/Lev26:23) · [PSA 2:10](https://ref.ly/Ps2:10) · [JER 6:8](https://ref.ly/Jer6:8) · [JER 31:18](https://ref.ly/Jer31:18) · [EZK 23:48](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:48)

Unsafe

to scourge \= action by which humans in a position of authority treat their subjects with aggression and harshness

[1KI 12:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:11) · [1KI 12:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:11) · [1KI 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:14) · [1KI 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:14) · [2CH 10:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:11) · [2CH 10:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:14)

Urge

to charge \= causative action by which deities use strong persuasion to cause humans to adhere to a prescribed action

[ISA 8:11](https://ref.ly/Isa8:11)

Control

to direct, to be in charge \= state in which humans are in control of a group of people by virtue of knowledge or position

[1CH 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:22)1

Know

to instruct \= causative action by which humans or deities cause humans to possess particular knowledge

[DEU 4:36](https://ref.ly/Deut4:36) · [PSA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ps16:7) · [PSA 94:12](https://ref.ly/Ps94:12) · [PRO 31:1](https://ref.ly/Prov31:1) · [ISA 28:26](https://ref.ly/Isa28:26)

verb

Strong

**Collocation:** יסר זְרֹועֹת

to strengthen literally: to strengthen (someone's) arms; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause nations to be powerful

[HOS 7:15](https://ref.ly/Hos7:15)

Confident

to strengthen \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to be mentally strong

[JOB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Job4:3)

1 1CH 15:22 · HOS 10:10 MT \= IA. Targum, Vulgate, some Greek understands qal impf 3ms from שׂרר "rule, have oversight"

## יָעֵל (id: 003001000000000)

יָעֵל
=====

יָעֵל (H3277 · H3278)

noun m · יַעֲלָה

Wild Animals

ibex, wild goat, mountain goat \= fairly large wild goat, with adults being about ninety centimeters high at the shoulder; for most of the year its color is grayish but it turns browner in winter; males have thick long horns over 130 centimeters in length which curve backwards in a semicircle; the horns of the females are much slimmer and shorter, reaching a length of only about 40 centimeters; only the last few centimeters of the horns are smooth, the rest being ringed with ridges; these animals live in small herds in the mountains and prefer cliffs and crags, where they live off the bushes that grow on the ledges; ► clean animal, so may be eaten

[JOB 39:1](https://ref.ly/Job39:1) · [PSA 104:18](https://ref.ly/Ps104:18)

noun m

Names of Landforms

**Collocation:** צוּרֵי הַיְּעֵלִים

Rocks of Wild Goats \= rocks; ◄ located near En\-Gedi; ► one of the places where David and his men were hiding for Saul

[1SA 24:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:3)

name

Names of People

Jael \= woman; ◄ wife of Heber, Kenite; ► time of the Judges

## יָעֵן (id: 003006000000000)

יָעֵן
=====

יָעֵן (H3283)

noun m

Birds

ostrich \= large bird with underdeveloped wings so that itcannot fly; it has a long neck and long legs and is about 2 meters tall; its head and neck are almost bald, being covered in short bristles; the male's body is black with long white feathers on its wings; its tail is white, gray, or light brown; the females are a uniform grayish brown; they feed mainly on seeds, fallen fruit, and shoots and may occasionally eat a lizard or a frog; they live in small flocks of about twenty, of which four or five are males; as many as ten females may lay their eggs in the one nest, but only one female and one male will incubate the eggs, with the female on the nest in the daytime, and the male taking over later in the afternoon; male ostriches make a series of deep booming hoots that can be heard great distances and which are often mistaken for the roar of a lion; ◄ \|iStruthio camelus; \|i\*≈ associated with heartless cruelty because most females do not hatch their own eggs and do not take care of their own young

[LAM 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lam4:3)

1 LAM 4:3 MT\-Q כַּיְעֵנִים \| MT\-K ענים

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Lamentations 4:3


## יַעֲנָה (id: 003007000000000)

יַעֲנָה
=======

יַעֲנָה (H3284) — בַּת יַעֲנָה

noun f

Birds

eagle owl exact meaning unknown; probably: \= a type of owl, large, standing over 75 centimeters (30 inches) tall; it has a pale fawn color, is spotted, has ear tufts, and is best known by its loud, deep hooting at night; it roosts by day in deep shade in acacia trees, caves, tombs, and ruined buildings; it feeds on small mammals, including hares, baby gazelles, lambs, rats and mice, and large roosting birds, especially wild and domestic ducks; ◄ Bubo bubo; ► unclean and therefore not to be eaten

Think

to take counsel, to seek advice \= action by which humans come together to exercise their minds as a group to determine how they are going to achieve a particular purpose

## יַעַר (id: 003016000000000)

יַעַר
=====

יַעַר (H3293 · H3297) — יַעֲרָה, יְעָרִים

noun m · קִרְיַת יְעָרִים

Landscapes

forest, thickets, woods \= a large group of trees or shrubs which grow close together and where several wild animals are known to live; ≈ considered to be a dangerous place for humans and domestic animals

[DEU 19:5](https://ref.ly/Deut19:5) · [JOS 17:15](https://ref.ly/Josh17:15) · [JOS 17:18](https://ref.ly/Josh17:18) · [1SA 14:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:25) · [1SA 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:26) · [1SA 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:5) · [2SA 18:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:6) · [2SA 18:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:8) · [2SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:17) · [2KI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:24) · [2KI 19:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:23) · [1CH 16:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:33) · [PSA 29:9](https://ref.ly/Ps29:9) · [PSA 50:10](https://ref.ly/Ps50:10) · [PSA 80:14](https://ref.ly/Ps80:14) · [PSA 83:15](https://ref.ly/Ps83:15) · [PSA 96:12](https://ref.ly/Ps96:12) · [PSA 104:20](https://ref.ly/Ps104:20) · [ECC 2:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:6) · [SNG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Song2:3) · [ISA 7:2](https://ref.ly/Isa7:2) · [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17) · [ISA 10:18](https://ref.ly/Isa10:18) · [ISA 10:19](https://ref.ly/Isa10:19) · [ISA 10:34](https://ref.ly/Isa10:34) · [ISA 21:13](https://ref.ly/Isa21:13) · [ISA 29:17](https://ref.ly/Isa29:17) · [ISA 32:15](https://ref.ly/Isa32:15) · [ISA 32:19](https://ref.ly/Isa32:19) · [ISA 37:24](https://ref.ly/Isa37:24) · [ISA 44:14](https://ref.ly/Isa44:14) · [ISA 44:23](https://ref.ly/Isa44:23) · [ISA 56:9](https://ref.ly/Isa56:9) · [JER 5:6](https://ref.ly/Jer5:6) · [JER 10:3](https://ref.ly/Jer10:3) · [JER 12:8](https://ref.ly/Jer12:8) · [JER 21:14](https://ref.ly/Jer21:14) · [JER 26:18](https://ref.ly/Jer26:18) · [JER 46:23](https://ref.ly/Jer46:23) · [EZK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek15:2) · [EZK 15:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek15:6) · [EZK 21:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:2) · [EZK 21:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:3) · [EZK 34:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:25) · [EZK 39:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:10) · [HOS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14) · [AMO 3:4](https://ref.ly/Amos3:4) · [MIC 3:12](https://ref.ly/Mic3:12) · [MIC 5:7](https://ref.ly/Mic5:7) · [MIC 7:14](https://ref.ly/Mic7:14) · [ZEC 11:2](https://ref.ly/Zech11:2)

Names of Constructions

**Collocation:** בֵית יַעַר הלְּבָנֹון

House of the Forest of Lebanon literally: "House of the Forest of Lebanon" \= a very large building in Jerusalem built by King Solomon, which consisted of four rows of pillars of cedar wood; this cedar wood likely came from the forests of Lebanon, located north of Israel

[1KI 7:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:2) · [1KI 10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:17) · [1KI 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:21) · [2CH 9:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:16) · [2CH 9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:20) · [ISA 22:8](https://ref.ly/Isa22:8)

Names of Locations

Jaar \= town, also known as Kiriath\-jearim, Kiriath\-Baal, Baalah, and Baale\-Judah; ◄ where the ark of covenant was kept before David took it to Jerusalem

[PSA 132:6](https://ref.ly/Ps132:6)

Names of Landforms

**Collocation:** הַר־יְעָרִים

Mount Jearim \= a mountain between Kiriath\-Jearim and Beth\-Shemesh

[JOS 15:10](https://ref.ly/Josh15:10)

noun m

Food

honeycomb \= a structure of hexagonal cells of wax, made by bees to store honey and eggs, ► which is eaten, together with the honey; ≈ regarded as sweet and tasty

[1SA 14:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:27) · [SNG 5:1](https://ref.ly/Song5:1)

Anne Garber Kompaore

## יפע (id: 003034000000000)

יפע
===

יפע (H3313)

verb · יִפְעָה

Shine

to shine, to shine forth \= process by which a light bearing object gives out or reflects a bright light

[JOB 3:4](https://ref.ly/Job3:4) · [JOB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Job10:22)

Shine

to make shine \= process by which some light source or luminous object is caused to send light

[JOB 37:15](https://ref.ly/Job37:15)

Willing

to smile literally: to shine (on); hence: \= action by which deities look on something with approval, ◄ as if their eyes shine upon it

[JOB 10:3](https://ref.ly/Job10:3)

Space

to shine forth literally: to shine (forth); hence: \= process by which a deity appears accompanied by a bright shining light

[DEU 33:2](https://ref.ly/Deut33:2) · [PSA 50:2](https://ref.ly/Ps50:2) · [PSA 80:2](https://ref.ly/Ps80:2) · [PSA 94:1](https://ref.ly/Ps94:1)

1 PSA 50:2 RSV NRSV NIV NJB REB GNT "shine forth" \| NJPS "appear" 2 PSA 80:2 RSV NRSV NIV REB NJB GNT "shine forth" \| NJPS "appear" 3 PSA 94:1 RSV NRSV NIV NJB GNT "shine forth" \| NJPS "appear" (REB "show yourself") 4 DEU 33:2 RSV NRSV NIV GNT CEV "shine forth" (NJB) \| NJPS REB "appear"

Enio R. Mueller
