Afraid

**Collocation:** לֵב יצא

to lose heart literally: (one's) heart goes out; hence: \= process by which humans lose courage and become afraid

[GEN 42:28](https://ref.ly/Gen42:28)

Afraid

**Collocation:** נֶפֶשׁ יצא

to lose heart literally: (one's) self goes out; hence: \= process by which humans lose courage and become afraid

[SNG 5:6](https://ref.ly/Song5:6)

Angry

**Collocation:** יצא רוּחַ

to vent (one's) anger literally: to bring out (one's) spirit; hence: \= action by which humans give full vent to their anger without holding anything back, ≈ which is regarded as a foolish deed

[PRO 29:11](https://ref.ly/Prov29:11)

Associate

to come out of (a group), to be one of a group literally: to come out (of a group); hence: \= process by which humans are a member of a certain community

[JER 30:21](https://ref.ly/Jer30:21) · [MIC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Mic5:1) · [NAM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Nah1:11) · [ZEC 10:4](https://ref.ly/Zech10:4)

Attach

to go out literally: to go out from; hence: \= state in which part of an object is connected to the object itself in such as way that it seems as if it has grown out of it

[EXO 25:32](https://ref.ly/Exod25:32) · [EXO 25:33](https://ref.ly/Exod25:33) · [EXO 25:35](https://ref.ly/Exod25:35) · [EXO 37:18](https://ref.ly/Exod37:18) · [EXO 37:19](https://ref.ly/Exod37:19) · [EXO 37:21](https://ref.ly/Exod37:21)

Birth

to come forth \= process of conception, described as going out from one's father's seed

[GEN 15:4](https://ref.ly/Gen15:4) · [GEN 17:6](https://ref.ly/Gen17:6) · [GEN 35:11](https://ref.ly/Gen35:11) · [GEN 46:26](https://ref.ly/Gen46:26) · [EXO 1:5](https://ref.ly/Exod1:5) · [JDG 8:30](https://ref.ly/Judg8:30) · [2SA 7:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:12) · [2SA 16:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:11) · [1KI 8:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:19) · [2KI 20:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:18) · [2CH 6:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:9) · [ISA 39:7](https://ref.ly/Isa39:7) · [ISA 48:1](https://ref.ly/Isa48:1)10

Birth

to come forth literally: to go out (from the womb); hence: \= process by which children are born

[GEN 25:25](https://ref.ly/Gen25:25) · [GEN 25:26](https://ref.ly/Gen25:26) · [GEN 38:28](https://ref.ly/Gen38:28) · [GEN 38:29](https://ref.ly/Gen38:29) · [GEN 38:30](https://ref.ly/Gen38:30) · [EXO 21:22](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22) · [DEU 28:57](https://ref.ly/Deut28:57) · [JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21) · [JOB 3:11](https://ref.ly/Job3:11) · [JOB 38:8](https://ref.ly/Job38:8) · [JOB 38:29](https://ref.ly/Job38:29) · [ECC 5:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:14) · [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5) · [JER 20:18](https://ref.ly/Jer20:18)

Birth

to bring forth literally: to bring out (from the womb); hence: \= causative process by which deities cause children to be born

[JOB 10:18](https://ref.ly/Job10:18) · [ISA 65:9](https://ref.ly/Isa65:9)

Chastise

**Collocation:** יָד יצא

to chastise literally: (God's) hand goes out (against someone); hence: \= action by which a deities chastise humans ◄ as if by striking them physically with their hands

[RUT 1:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:13)

Complete

to be completed \= process by which a construction project comes to an end

[EZR 6:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:15)

Dead

**Collocation:** נֶפֶשׁ יצא

to breathe (one's) last, to die literally: (someone's) self goes out; hence: \= process by which humans breathe their last and die

[GEN 35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:18)

Complete

to end literally: (a year) goes out; hence: \= process by which by which a period of time comes to an end

[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)

Engage

to engage, to involve oneself literally: to go out (to do something); hence: \= action by which humans engage in a certain activity

[PRO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Prov25:8)

Engage

**Collocation:** יצא צָבָא

to go out literally: to go out as army; \= state in which men qualify for military service

[NUM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:3) · [NUM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Num1:20) · [NUM 1:22](https://ref.ly/Num1:22) · [NUM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Num1:24) · [NUM 1:26](https://ref.ly/Num1:26) · [NUM 1:28](https://ref.ly/Num1:28) · [NUM 1:30](https://ref.ly/Num1:30) · [NUM 1:32](https://ref.ly/Num1:32) · [NUM 1:34](https://ref.ly/Num1:34) · [NUM 1:36](https://ref.ly/Num1:36) · [NUM 1:38](https://ref.ly/Num1:38) · [NUM 1:40](https://ref.ly/Num1:40) · [NUM 1:42](https://ref.ly/Num1:42) · [NUM 1:45](https://ref.ly/Num1:45) · [NUM 26:2](https://ref.ly/Num26:2) · [1CH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:18) · [1CH 7:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:11) · [1CH 12:34](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:34) · [1CH 12:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:37) · [2CH 25:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5) · [2CH 26:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:11)

Non\-Engage

to escape literally: to go out (from a circumstance); hence: \= process by which humans are no longer affected by a certain circumstance

[PRO 12:13](https://ref.ly/Prov12:13) · [ECC 7:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:18) · [JER 11:11](https://ref.ly/Jer11:11)

Non\-Engage

to help escape literally: to bring out (of a circumstance); hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to be no longer affected by a certain circumstance

[PSA 25:17](https://ref.ly/Ps25:17) · [PSA 107:28](https://ref.ly/Ps107:28) · [PSA 143:11](https://ref.ly/Ps143:11)

Excrete

to go out \= process by which a substance such as food or semen leaves the body through one of its orifices

[LEV 15:16](https://ref.ly/Lev15:16) · [LEV 15:32](https://ref.ly/Lev15:32) · [LEV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4) · [NUM 11:20](https://ref.ly/Num11:20)

Exist

to come out literally: to go out from; hence: \= process by which a certain procedure results in a new product

[EXO 32:24](https://ref.ly/Exod32:24) · [PRO 25:4](https://ref.ly/Prov25:4)

Exist

to produce literally: to bring out from; hence: \= causative process by which humans carry out a certain procedure which results in a new product

[EXO 8:14](https://ref.ly/Exod8:14) · [PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33) · [ISA 54:16](https://ref.ly/Isa54:16)

Non\-Exist

to leave, to go out, to vanish literally: to go out; hence: \= process by which an object vanishes from existence

[1SA 25:37](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:37) · [EZK 26:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:18)

Flow

to come forth, to flow out literally: to come out (of a certain place); hence: \= process by which liquids flow from a certain place

[NUM 20:11](https://ref.ly/Num20:11) · [DEU 8:7](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7) · [JDG 15:19](https://ref.ly/Judg15:19) · [EZK 47:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:1) · [EZK 47:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:8) · [EZK 47:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:12) · [JOL 4:18](https://ref.ly/Joel4:18) · [ZEC 14:8](https://ref.ly/Zech14:8)

Flow

to bring out, to cause to flow literally: to bring out (of a certain place); hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause liquids to flow from a certain place

[NUM 20:8](https://ref.ly/Num20:8) · [NUM 20:10](https://ref.ly/Num20:10) · [DEU 8:15](https://ref.ly/Deut8:15) · [PSA 78:16](https://ref.ly/Ps78:16) · [EZK 47:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:8)

Free

to be released literally: to go out; hence: \= process by which a person or object is no longer under someone else's control

[EXO 21:2](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2) · [LEV 25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:28) · [LEV 25:30](https://ref.ly/Lev25:30) · [LEV 25:31](https://ref.ly/Lev25:31) · [LEV 25:33](https://ref.ly/Lev25:33) · [LEV 27:21](https://ref.ly/Lev27:21)

Guilty

**Collocation:** יצא רָשָׁע

to be found guilty literally: to come out guilty; hence: \= process by which humans are found guilty in court

[PSA 109:7](https://ref.ly/Ps109:7)

Happen

to come (from), to go forth literally: to go out; hence: \= process by which an event starts taking place, originating from a certain deity, person, or place

[GEN 24:50](https://ref.ly/Gen24:50) · [NUM 17:11](https://ref.ly/Num17:11) · [1SA 24:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:14) · [JOB 5:6](https://ref.ly/Job5:6) · [PSA 17:2](https://ref.ly/Ps17:2) · [PSA 19:5](https://ref.ly/Ps19:5) · [ECC 10:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:5) · [ISA 28:29](https://ref.ly/Isa28:29) · [ISA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Isa51:5) · [JER 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jer4:4) · [JER 9:2](https://ref.ly/Jer9:2) · [JER 21:12](https://ref.ly/Jer21:12) · [JER 23:15](https://ref.ly/Jer23:15) · [JER 23:19](https://ref.ly/Jer23:19) · [JER 25:32](https://ref.ly/Jer25:32) · [JER 30:19](https://ref.ly/Jer30:19) · [JER 30:23](https://ref.ly/Jer30:23) · [EZK 7:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:10) · [HOS 6:5](https://ref.ly/Hos6:5) · [HAB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:4) · [HAB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:4) · [HAB 1:7](https://ref.ly/Hab1:7) · [HAB 3:5](https://ref.ly/Hab3:5) · [ZEC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3)

Happen

to bring about literally: to bring out; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause an event to start taking place

[PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33) · [PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33) · [ISA 42:1](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1) · [ISA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Isa42:3) · [JER 51:10](https://ref.ly/Jer51:10) · [ZEC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Zech5:4)

End

to go out, to depart literally: to go out; hence: \= process by which an event stops taking place

[PRO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Prov22:10) · [LAM 1:6](https://ref.ly/Lam1:6)

Know

to go out, to go forth, to be known literally: to go out; hence: \= process by which certain information is becoming publicly known

[1CH 14:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:17) · [2CH 26:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:15) · [EST 1:17](https://ref.ly/Esth1:17) · [EST 1:19](https://ref.ly/Esth1:19) · [ISA 2:3](https://ref.ly/Isa2:3) · [ISA 51:4](https://ref.ly/Isa51:4) · [ISA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Isa62:1) · [EZK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:14) · [DAN 9:23](https://ref.ly/Dan9:23) · [MIC 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mic4:2)

Know

to send out, to proclaim literally: to bring out; hence: \= causative process by which certain information is made publicly known

[NEH 6:19](https://ref.ly/Neh6:19) · [PSA 37:6](https://ref.ly/Ps37:6) · [ISA 48:20](https://ref.ly/Isa48:20)

Know

**Collocation:** יצא דִּבָּה

to bring out a bad report literally: to bring out a bad report; hence: \= causative process by which humans publish bad information about something

[NUM 13:32](https://ref.ly/Num13:32) · [NUM 14:36](https://ref.ly/Num14:36) · [NUM 14:37](https://ref.ly/Num14:37) · [PRO 10:18](https://ref.ly/Prov10:18)

Know

**Collocation:** יצא שֵׁם רָע

to give (someone) a bad name literally: to bring out a bad name; hence: \= causative process by which humans say certain things about other humans that give them a bad reputation

[DEU 22:14](https://ref.ly/Deut22:14) · [DEU 22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:19)

Know

**Collocation:** גֹורָל יצא

the lots falls to (someone) literally: the lot comes out; hence: \= process by which the lot has been thrown and the outcome has become clear

[NUM 33:54](https://ref.ly/Num33:54) · [JOS 16:1](https://ref.ly/Josh16:1) · [JOS 16:2](https://ref.ly/Josh16:2) · [JOS 19:1](https://ref.ly/Josh19:1) · [JOS 19:17](https://ref.ly/Josh19:17) · [JOS 19:24](https://ref.ly/Josh19:24) · [JOS 19:32](https://ref.ly/Josh19:32) · [JOS 19:40](https://ref.ly/Josh19:40) · [JOS 21:4](https://ref.ly/Josh21:4) · [1CH 24:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:7) · [1CH 25:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:9) · [1CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:14)

Possess

to expend, to pay literally: to go out; hence: \= process by which money is spent on a certain commodity

[2KI 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:13)

Possess

to be imported literally: to go out (from a location); hence: \= process by which a certain commodity is purchased in a foreign location to be used locally

[1KI 10:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:29)

Possess

to export literally: to bring out (from a location); hence: \= causative process by which humans sell a certain commodity to be used in a foreign location

[1KI 10:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:29)

Non\-Possess

to lose (possession) literally: (someone's property) goes out; hence: \= process by which humans lose possession of something

[JOS 19:47](https://ref.ly/Josh19:47)

See

**Collocation:** יצא לָאוֹר

to bring to light literally: to bring to light; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause something that was previously hidden to become fully visible

[JOB 12:22](https://ref.ly/Job12:22) · [MIC 7:9](https://ref.ly/Mic7:9)

Shape

to project, to protrude, to extend literally: to come out; hence: \= state in which an object extends into a certain direction

[NUM 21:13](https://ref.ly/Num21:13) · [NEH 3:25](https://ref.ly/Neh3:25) · [NEH 3:26](https://ref.ly/Neh3:26) · [NEH 3:27](https://ref.ly/Neh3:27) · [PSA 73:7](https://ref.ly/Ps73:7)

Space

to go out literally: (a boundary or measuring line) goes out; hence: \= state in which a boundary or measuring line follows a certain trajectory

[NUM 34:4](https://ref.ly/Num34:4) · [NUM 34:9](https://ref.ly/Num34:9) · [JOS 15:3](https://ref.ly/Josh15:3) · [JOS 15:4](https://ref.ly/Josh15:4) · [JOS 15:9](https://ref.ly/Josh15:9) · [JOS 15:11](https://ref.ly/Josh15:11) · [JOS 15:11](https://ref.ly/Josh15:11) · [JOS 16:6](https://ref.ly/Josh16:6) · [JOS 16:7](https://ref.ly/Josh16:7) · [JOS 18:11](https://ref.ly/Josh18:11) · [JOS 18:15](https://ref.ly/Josh18:15) · [JOS 18:15](https://ref.ly/Josh18:15) · [JOS 18:17](https://ref.ly/Josh18:17) · [JOS 18:17](https://ref.ly/Josh18:17) · [JOS 19:12](https://ref.ly/Josh19:12) · [JOS 19:13](https://ref.ly/Josh19:13) · [JOS 19:27](https://ref.ly/Josh19:27) · [JOS 19:34](https://ref.ly/Josh19:34) · [JER 31:39](https://ref.ly/Jer31:39)

Speak

to be uttered, to be spoken literally: (word) come out of (someone); hence: \= process by which words are spoken by humans or deities

[NUM 30:3](https://ref.ly/Num30:3) · [NUM 32:24](https://ref.ly/Num32:24) · [JOS 6:10](https://ref.ly/Josh6:10) · [JDG 11:36](https://ref.ly/Judg11:36) · [1SA 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:3) · [EST 7:8](https://ref.ly/Esth7:8) · [JOB 37:2](https://ref.ly/Job37:2) · [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23) · [ISA 48:3](https://ref.ly/Isa48:3) · [ISA 55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:11) · [JER 44:17](https://ref.ly/Jer44:17) · [LAM 3:38](https://ref.ly/Lam3:38) · [EZK 33:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:30)

Speak

to utter literally: to bring out; hence: \= action by which humans utter words

[JOB 8:10](https://ref.ly/Job8:10) · [JOB 15:13](https://ref.ly/Job15:13) · [ECC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1) · [JER 15:19](https://ref.ly/Jer15:19)

Stage

to go out, to grow out literally: to come out; \= process by which land or a living organism produces crops, branches, horns, or fruits

[DEU 14:22](https://ref.ly/Deut14:22) · [JDG 13:14](https://ref.ly/Judg13:14) · [1KI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:13) · [JOB 8:16](https://ref.ly/Job8:16) · [JOB 14:2](https://ref.ly/Job14:2) · [JOB 31:40](https://ref.ly/Job31:40) · [ISA 11:1](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1) · [ISA 14:29](https://ref.ly/Isa14:29) · [DAN 8:9](https://ref.ly/Dan8:9)

Stage

to bring forth literally: to bring out; \= causative process by which deities cause land, or objects to produce flowers or crops

## יצב (id: 003040000000000)

יצב
===

יצב (H3320 · A3321)

verb · יַצִּיב

Space

to take position \= action by which humans or deities take position in a certain place for a specific purpose

[EXO 2:4](https://ref.ly/Exod2:4) · [EXO 8:16](https://ref.ly/Exod8:16) · [EXO 9:13](https://ref.ly/Exod9:13) · [EXO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Exod19:17) · [EXO 34:5](https://ref.ly/Exod34:5) · [NUM 11:16](https://ref.ly/Num11:16) · [NUM 22:22](https://ref.ly/Num22:22) · [NUM 23:3](https://ref.ly/Num23:3) · [NUM 23:15](https://ref.ly/Num23:15) · [DEU 7:24](https://ref.ly/Deut7:24) · [DEU 31:14](https://ref.ly/Deut31:14) · [DEU 31:14](https://ref.ly/Deut31:14) · [JOS 24:1](https://ref.ly/Josh24:1) · [JDG 20:2](https://ref.ly/Judg20:2) · [1SA 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:10) · [1SA 10:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:19) · [1SA 10:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:23) · [1SA 12:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:7) · [1SA 12:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:16) · [1SA 17:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:16) · [2SA 18:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:13) · [2SA 18:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:30) · [2SA 23:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:12) · [1CH 11:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:14) · [2CH 11:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:13) · [2CH 20:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:17) · [JOB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Job1:6) · [JOB 2:1](https://ref.ly/Job2:1) · [JOB 2:1](https://ref.ly/Job2:1) · [JOB 33:5](https://ref.ly/Job33:5) · [PSA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2) · [PRO 22:29](https://ref.ly/Prov22:29) · [PRO 22:29](https://ref.ly/Prov22:29) · [JER 46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:4) · [JER 46:14](https://ref.ly/Jer46:14) · [HAB 2:1](https://ref.ly/Hab2:1) · [ZEC 6:5](https://ref.ly/Zech6:5)

Act

**Collocation:** יצב עַל־דֶּרֶךְ

to commit oneself to a course literally: to take position on a road; hence: \= action by which humans engage in a certain pattern of behavior

[PSA 36:5](https://ref.ly/Ps36:5)

Confident

to stand firm literally: to take position; hence: \= action by which humans stand firm without fear in the face of danger

[EXO 14:13](https://ref.ly/Exod14:13) · [DEU 9:2](https://ref.ly/Deut9:2) · [DEU 11:25](https://ref.ly/Deut11:25) · [JOS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Josh1:5) · [2CH 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:6) · [JOB 41:2](https://ref.ly/Job41:2) · [PSA 5:6](https://ref.ly/Ps5:6) · [PSA 94:16](https://ref.ly/Ps94:16)

Know

to ascertain literally: to cause to stand firm; hence: \= action by which humans want to have reliable information about something

[DAN 7:19](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19)

Dwell

to have a place (somewhere) literally: to take position; hence: \= state in which a group of people is allowed to dwell in a certain area

[2SA 21:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:5)

See

to stand out literally: to take position; hence: \= process by which certain features of objects become clearly visible

[JOB 38:14](https://ref.ly/Job38:14)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 2:4; Exodus 8:16; Exodus 9:13; Exodus 19:17; Exodus 34:5; Numbers 11:16; Numbers 22:22; Numbers 23:3; Numbers 23:15; Deuteronomy 7:24; Deuteronomy 31:14; Joshua 24:1; Judges 20:2; 1 Samuel 3:10; 1 Samuel 10:19; 1 Samuel 10:23; 1 Samuel 12:7; 1 Samuel 12:16; 1 Samuel 17:16; 2 Samuel 18:13; 2 Samuel 18:30; 2 Samuel 23:12; 1 Chronicles 11:14; 2 Chronicles 11:13; 2 Chronicles 20:17; Job 1:6; Job 2:1; Job 33:5; Psalms 2:2; Proverbs 22:29; Jeremiah 46:4; Jeremiah 46:14; Habakkuk 2:1; Zechariah 6:5; Psalms 36:5; Exodus 14:13; Deuteronomy 9:2; Deuteronomy 11:25; Joshua 1:5; 2 Chronicles 20:6; Job 41:2; Psalms 5:6; Psalms 94:16; Daniel 7:19; 2 Samuel 21:5; Job 38:14


## יצג (id: 003041000000000)

יצג
===

יצג (H3322)

verb

Space

to place, to set, to lay \= causative action by which humans place other humans or objects in a certain location

[GEN 30:38](https://ref.ly/Gen30:38) · [GEN 33:15](https://ref.ly/Gen33:15) · [GEN 43:9](https://ref.ly/Gen43:9) · [GEN 47:2](https://ref.ly/Gen47:2) · [DEU 28:56](https://ref.ly/Deut28:56) · [JDG 6:37](https://ref.ly/Judg6:37) · [JDG 7:5](https://ref.ly/Judg7:5) · [JDG 8:27](https://ref.ly/Judg8:27) · [1SA 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:2) · [2SA 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:17) · [1CH 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:1) · [JER 51:34](https://ref.ly/Jer51:34) · [HOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hos2:5)

Space

to leave behind \= causative action by which humans cause objects to remain in a certain place

[EXO 10:24](https://ref.ly/Exod10:24)

Happen

to establish literally: to place; hence: \= causative action by which humans implement a certain activity

[AMO 5:15](https://ref.ly/Amos5:15)

Revile

**Collocation:** יצג לִמְשֹׁל

to make (someone) a byword literally: to place as a byword; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to be the object of ridicule

[JOB 17:6](https://ref.ly/Job17:6)

1 JER 51:34 MT\-K הציגנו \| MT\-Q הִצִּיגַנִי

Enio R. Mueller

## יִצְהָר (id: 003042000000000)

יִצְהָר
=======

יִצְהָר (H3323 · H3324)

noun m · צהר

Liquids

olive oil \= high quality oil, ◄ extracted from the fruits of the olive tree during the first pressing; ► used as food and in various religious rituals

[NUM 18:12](https://ref.ly/Num18:12) · [DEU 7:13](https://ref.ly/Deut7:13) · [DEU 11:14](https://ref.ly/Deut11:14) · [DEU 12:17](https://ref.ly/Deut12:17) · [DEU 14:23](https://ref.ly/Deut14:23) · [DEU 18:4](https://ref.ly/Deut18:4) · [DEU 28:51](https://ref.ly/Deut28:51) · [2KI 18:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:32) · [2CH 31:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:5) · [2CH 32:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:28) · [NEH 5:11](https://ref.ly/Neh5:11) · [NEH 10:38](https://ref.ly/Neh10:38) · [NEH 10:40](https://ref.ly/Neh10:40) · [NEH 13:5](https://ref.ly/Neh13:5) · [NEH 13:12](https://ref.ly/Neh13:12) · [JER 31:12](https://ref.ly/Jer31:12) · [HOS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Hos2:10) · [HOS 2:24](https://ref.ly/Hos2:24) · [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10) · [JOL 2:19](https://ref.ly/Joel2:19) · [JOL 2:24](https://ref.ly/Joel2:24) · [HAG 1:11](https://ref.ly/Hag1:11) · [ZEC 4:14](https://ref.ly/Zech4:14)

name · יִצְהָרִי

Names of People

Izhar \= man; ◄ son of Kohath; tribe of Levi; ► founder of Izharite clan

[EXO 6:18](https://ref.ly/Exod6:18) · [EXO 6:21](https://ref.ly/Exod6:21) · [NUM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Num3:19) · [NUM 16:1](https://ref.ly/Num16:1) · [1CH 5:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:28) · [1CH 6:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:3) · [1CH 6:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:23) · [1CH 23:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:12) · [1CH 23:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:18)

יִצְהָרִי
=========

יִצְהָרִי (H3325)

adj · יִצְהָר

Names of Groups

Izharite \= descendant of Izhar; ◄ subclan of Kohathites; tribe of Levi

## יָצוּעַ (id: 003044000000000)

יָצוּעַ
=======

יָצוּעַ (H3326a)

noun m · יצע

Furnishings

couch, bed \= a low platform standing on four legs, a bit longer than the length of a man, and about 70–80 centimeters wide; ► used by people of means to sleep on

[PSA 63:7](https://ref.ly/Ps63:7) · [PSA 132:3](https://ref.ly/Ps132:3)

Dead

**Collocation:** רפד יָצוּעַ בַּחֹשֶׁךְ

to make one's bed in darkness literally: to spreak (one's) bed in darkness; hence: \= process by which humans die and move to the world of the dead

[JOB 17:13](https://ref.ly/Job17:13)

Copulate

**Collocation:** עלה יְצוּעַ

to have sex with (someone else's) wife literally: to climb on (someone else's bed); hence: \= action by which humans have sexual intercourse with the wife or concubine of someone else, ≈ which is regarded as a defilement and hence a serious sin

[GEN 49:4](https://ref.ly/Gen49:4)

Copulate

**Collocation:** חלל יְצוּעַ

to have sex with (someone else's) wife literally: to defile (someone's) bed; hence: \= action by which humans have sexual intercourse with the wife or concubine of someone else, ≈ which is regarded as a defilement and hence a serious sin

[1CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1)

1 1KI 6:5 MT\-K \| MT\-Q "annex"

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 63:7; Psalms 132:3; Job 17:13; Genesis 49:4; 1 Chronicles 5:1

## יקע (id: 003077000000000)

יקע
===

יקע (H3363)

verb · נקע

Detach

to be dislocated, to be put out of joint \= process by which the position of a bone in its joint is disturbed, ► which results in the inability to use that bone properly

[GEN 32:26](https://ref.ly/Gen32:26)

Dead

to hang, to throw from a rock, to impale, to dismember exact meaning unsure, with several possibilities: \= causative action by which humans put other humans to death, though the exact way is unsure; possibly interpretations include (1\) hanging the body from a gibbet which results in dislocating some of the bones, (2\) throwing the body down from a rock which results in dislocating some of the bones, (3\) placing the body in a vertically position on a sharpened upright pole that will pierce it, (4\) cutting the body to pieces

[NUM 25:4](https://ref.ly/Num25:4)1 · [2SA 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:6)2 · [2SA 21:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:9)2 · [2SA 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:13)

Hate

to be disgusted \= process by which deities develop a strong aversion against (other) humans, often as a result of their behavior

[JER 6:8](https://ref.ly/Jer6:8)

1 NUM 25:4 RSV ESV "hang" NIV "kill and expose" \| NJPS "have them impaled" NJB NRSV "impale" \| REB "hurl down to death" \| GNT "execute" CEV "have killed" 2 2SA 21:6 · 2SA 21:9 RSV GNT ESV CEV "hang" NIV "to be killed and exposed" \| NJPS "impale" NRSV "impale" \| REB "hurl down to death" \| NJB "dismember"

Reinier de Blois

Afraid

fearful, awesome \= state in which humans, deities, places and activities have qualities that inspire fear in others

Afraid

to make afraid \= causative action by which humans inspire fear in other humans

ירד
===

verb · מֹורָד

Bear

**Collocation:** ירד עַל־קָדְקֹד

to come down on one's skull literally: to descend on one's skull; hence: \= state in which humans are held responsible for their deeds

Dead

to go down (to the netherworld), to die literally: to go down (to the netherworld, death, the pit, the dust, silence, etc.); hence: \= process by which humans die and descend into the world of the dead

Dead

to bring down, to cast down, to thrust down literally: to cause to go down (to the netherworld, death, the pit, the dust, silence, etc.); hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause humans to die and descend into the world of the dead

Dead

**Collocation:** ירד שֵׂיבָה

to bring (someone's) grey head down, to send down (someone's) grey head literally: to cause or allow someone's grey hair to descend into the netherworld; hence: \= causative process by which humans cause or allow an old person to die, usually under difficult circumstances

Flow

to flow down \= process by which bodies of water flow from a higher elevation to a lower elevation

Flow

to let flow down, to let run down, to let stream down \= causative process by which humans or deities cause bodies of water to flow from a higher elevation to a lower elevation

Grieve

**Collocation:** עֵנַיִם ירד

to weep profusely \= process by which tears flow in copious amounts from the eyes of humans ◄ as a result of deep sorrow

[PSA 119:136](https://ref.ly/Ps119:136) · [JER 9:17](https://ref.ly/Jer9:17) · [JER 13:17](https://ref.ly/Jer13:17) · [JER 14:17](https://ref.ly/Jer14:17) · [LAM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Lam1:16) · [LAM 3:48](https://ref.ly/Lam3:48)

Grieve

**Collocation:** ירד בַּבֶּכִי

to stream with tears, to melt in tears literally: to flow in weeping; hence: \= process by which tears flow in copious amounts from the eyes of humans ◄ as a result of deep sorrow

[ISA 15:3](https://ref.ly/Isa15:3)

Happen

to come down, to descend \= process by which an event starts happening as ordained by a deity, ◄ as if the event were descending straight from heaven

[MIC 1:12](https://ref.ly/Mic1:12)

Impact

to come down literally: to come down; \= process by which a construction collapses, ◄ usually caused by human hands

[DEU 20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:20) · [DEU 28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:52)

Impact

to bring down literally: to bring down; \= causative action by which humans cause a construction to collapse to be taken apart

[NUM 10:17](https://ref.ly/Num10:17) · [PRO 21:22](https://ref.ly/Prov21:22) · [AMO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Amos3:11)

Lax

to be brought low \= causative process by which a condition is caused to decrease in intensity

[ZEC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Zech10:11)

Shape

to go down literally: (a boundary or measuring line) goes down; hence: \= state in which a boundary or measuring line follows a certain trajectory downhill

Shame

to sink (in status) literally: to go down; hence: \= process by which humans lose their power or social status

[DEU 28:43](https://ref.ly/Deut28:43) · [LAM 1:9](https://ref.ly/Lam1:9)

Shame

to bring down literally: to bring down; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to lose their power or social status

[2SA 22:48](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:48) · [PSA 56:8](https://ref.ly/Ps56:8) · [PSA 59:12](https://ref.ly/Ps59:12) · [ISA 10:13](https://ref.ly/Isa10:13)

Strong

**Collocation:** ירד מִן מִטָּה

to recover literally: to come down from (one's) bed; hence: \= process by which humans recover from sickness

## ירה (id: 003097000000000)

ירה
===

ירה (H3384a · H3384b) — ירהּ

verb · יֹורֶה · תֹּורָה

Move

to throw, to cast \= causative action by which humans or deities throw an object in a certain direction, often with force

[EXO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Exod15:4) · [JOB 30:19](https://ref.ly/Job30:19)

Move

to shoot (arrows) \= causative action by which humans launch arrows with the help of a bow

[1SA 20:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:20) · [1SA 20:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:36) · [1SA 20:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:36) · [1SA 20:37](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:37) · [2SA 11:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:20) · [2SA 11:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:24) · [2KI 13:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:17) · [2KI 13:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:17) · [2KI 19:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:32) · [2CH 26:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:15) · [2CH 35:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:23) · [PSA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Ps11:2) · [PSA 64:5](https://ref.ly/Ps64:5) · [PSA 64:5](https://ref.ly/Ps64:5) · [PSA 64:8](https://ref.ly/Ps64:8) · [PRO 26:18](https://ref.ly/Prov26:18) · [ISA 37:33](https://ref.ly/Isa37:33)

Dead

to be shot literally: to be shot; hence: \= causative action by which humans or animals are killed by arrows

[EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13) · [EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13)

Impact

to overthrow literally: to cast down; exact meaning unknown; probably: \= causative action by which humans bring destruction to an area

[NUM 21:30](https://ref.ly/Num21:30)1

Know

**Collocation:** ירה גֹּורָל

to cast lots literally: to cast the lot; hence: \= causative action by which humans obtain guidance by throwing lots

[JOS 18:6](https://ref.ly/Josh18:6)

Soldiers

archer \= soldier who shoots with bow and arrows

[1SA 31:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:3) · [1SA 31:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:3) · [2SA 11:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:24) · [1CH 10:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:3) · [1CH 10:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:3) · [2CH 35:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:23)

Space

to put in place, to set up literally: to cast; hence: \= causative action by which humans put an object in a certain location

[GEN 31:51](https://ref.ly/Gen31:51) · [JOB 38:6](https://ref.ly/Job38:6)

verb · יֹורֶה

Wet

to water \= causative process by which a place is made moist by rainwater

[HOS 6:3](https://ref.ly/Hos6:3)

Happen

to rain, to shower (upon) \= causative process by which deities cause an event to take place in abundance, ◄ as if it were a shower of rain

[HOS 10:12](https://ref.ly/Hos10:12)

Safe

to be refreshed \= causative process by which humans are made to prosper in life, ◄ described as being refreshed with rainwater

[PRO 11:25](https://ref.ly/Prov11:25)

verb

Know

to direct, to teach, to instruct, to show \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to acquire knowledge, a skill, or a certain type of behavior

## יְרַח (id: 003104000000000)

יְרַח
=====

יְרַח (A3393)

noun m

Time

month \= one of twelve fractions of time that make up a year in the Israelite (lunar) calendar; ◄ equivalent to a full lunar cycle

[EZR 6:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:15) · [DAN 4:26](https://ref.ly/Dan4:26)

Reinier de Blois

## יֶרַח (id: 003105000000000)

יֶרַח
=====

יֶרַח (H3391 · H3392)

noun m · יָרֵחַ

Time

month \= period of time, measured by a complete cycle in the phases of the moon

[EXO 2:2](https://ref.ly/Exod2:2) · [DEU 21:13](https://ref.ly/Deut21:13) · [DEU 33:14](https://ref.ly/Deut33:14)1 · [1KI 6:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:37) · [1KI 6:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:38) · [1KI 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:2) · [2KI 15:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:13) · [JOB 3:6](https://ref.ly/Job3:6) · [JOB 7:3](https://ref.ly/Job7:3) · [JOB 29:2](https://ref.ly/Job29:2) · [JOB 39:2](https://ref.ly/Job39:2) · [ZEC 11:8](https://ref.ly/Zech11:8)

name

Names of People

Jerah \= man; ◄ son of Joktan; descendant of Shem; ► regarded as founder of ethnic group with the same name

[GEN 10:26](https://ref.ly/Gen10:26) · [1CH 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:20)

## ירט (id: 003112000000000)

ירט
===

ירט (H3399)

verb

Move

to make straight (for something), to move recklessly meaning unsure; possibly: \= action by which humans move in a certain direction with speed and without thinking of the consequences

[NUM 22:32](https://ref.ly/Num22:32)1

Control

**Collocation:** ירט עַל־יָדַיִם

to thrust (someone) into the clutches (of someone else) exact meaning unsure; possibly: to cast (someone) forcibly on the hands (of someone else); hence: \= causative process by which deities use force to deliver humans in the hands of others

[JOB 16:11](https://ref.ly/Job16:11)

1 NUM 22:32 REB "you made straight for me" \| RSV NRSV ESV "your way is perverse before me" NIV "your path is a reckless one before me" NJPS "the errand is obnoxious to me" \| NJB "your road is blocked" \| Seebass "the way will become a scare before me" \= רטט

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 22:32; Job 16:11

## יָרֵךְ (id: 003121000000000)

יָרֵךְ
======

יָרֵךְ (H3409)

noun f · יְרֵכָה

thigh \= the upper part of the leg of humans and animals

[GEN 32:26](https://ref.ly/Gen32:26) · [GEN 32:26](https://ref.ly/Gen32:26) · [GEN 32:32](https://ref.ly/Gen32:32) · [GEN 32:33](https://ref.ly/Gen32:33) · [GEN 32:33](https://ref.ly/Gen32:33) · [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42) · [EXO 32:27](https://ref.ly/Exod32:27) · [JDG 3:16](https://ref.ly/Judg3:16) · [JDG 3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:21) · [PSA 45:4](https://ref.ly/Ps45:4) · [SNG 3:8](https://ref.ly/Song3:8) · [SNG 7:2](https://ref.ly/Song7:2) · [EZK 24:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:4)

Spatial Referents

base literally: thigh (of an object); hence: \= the part of an artifact that touches the ground

[EXO 25:31](https://ref.ly/Exod25:31) · [EXO 37:17](https://ref.ly/Exod37:17) · [NUM 8:4](https://ref.ly/Num8:4)

Spatial Referents

side \= a part of an object that faces one of the four cardinal directions

[EXO 40:22](https://ref.ly/Exod40:22) · [EXO 40:24](https://ref.ly/Exod40:24) · [LEV 1:11](https://ref.ly/Lev1:11) · [NUM 3:29](https://ref.ly/Num3:29) · [NUM 3:35](https://ref.ly/Num3:35) · [2KI 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:14)

People

private parts, sexual organs \= a euphemism for the sexual organs of both male and female ◄ because their location near the upper thigh

[GEN 24:2](https://ref.ly/Gen24:2) · [GEN 24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:9) · [GEN 46:26](https://ref.ly/Gen46:26) · [GEN 47:29](https://ref.ly/Gen47:29) · [EXO 1:5](https://ref.ly/Exod1:5) · [NUM 5:21](https://ref.ly/Num5:21) · [NUM 5:22](https://ref.ly/Num5:22) · [NUM 5:27](https://ref.ly/Num5:27) · [JDG 8:30](https://ref.ly/Judg8:30)

Impact

**Collocation:** נכה שֹׁוק עַל־יָרֵךְ

to give (someone) a thorough thrashing meaning unsure; literally: to strike (someone) thigh over thigh; possibly: \= action by which humans fight other humans and beat them down effectively

[JDG 15:8](https://ref.ly/Judg15:8)

Stance

**Collocation:** ספק אֶל־יָרֵךְ · ספק עַל־יָרֵךְ

to strike (one's) thigh literally: to strike (one's) thigh; hence: \= action by which humans strike their upper leg to give expression to their grief

[JER 31:19](https://ref.ly/Jer31:19) · [EZK 21:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:17)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 32:26; Genesis 32:32; Genesis 32:33; Exodus 28:42; Exodus 32:27; Judges 3:16; Judges 3:21; Psalms 45:4; Song of Songs 3:8; Song of Songs 7:2; Ezekiel 24:4; Exodus 25:31; Exodus 37:17; Numbers 8:4; Exodus 40:22; Exodus 40:24; Leviticus 1:11; Numbers 3:29; Numbers 3:35; 2 Kings 16:14; Genesis 24:2; Genesis 24:9; Genesis 46:26; Genesis 47:29; Exodus 1:5; Numbers 5:21; Numbers 5:22; Numbers 5:27; Judges 8:30; Judges 15:8; Jeremiah 31:19; Ezekiel 21:17

יְרֵשָׁה
========

יְרֵשָׁה (H3424)

noun f · ירשׁ

Land

possession (of someone else) \= an area that has become someone's possession, which often implies that this possession will be passed on to the next generation of the new owner

## יְרֻשָּׁה (id: 003138000000000)

יְרֻשָּׁה
=========

יְרֻשָּׁה (H3425 · H3388b)

noun f · ירשׁ

Land

possession (of someone else) \= an area that has become someone's possession, which often implies that this possession has been granted by God and that it will be passed on to the next generation of the new owner

Space · Stance

to make sit, to seat \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to assume a sitting position in a certain location and remain in that position for some time; ≈ sometimes associated with a certain status, role, or common activity

[1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8) · [1KI 21:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:9) · [1KI 21:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:10) · [1KI 21:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:12) · [PSA 113:8](https://ref.ly/Ps113:8)

Control · Stance

**Collocation:** ישׁב עַל כִּסֵּא

to be enthroned literally: to sit on a throne; hence: \= action by which humans or deities assume a position of leadership, ◄ symbolized by taking position on the seat that represents this position of authority

Control · Stance

**Collocation:** ישׁב עַל כִּסֵּא

to enthrone literally: to make (someone) sit on a throne; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to assume a position of leadership, ◄ symbolized by having them taking position on the seat that represents this position of authority

[1KI 2:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:24) · [2CH 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:20)

Control · Stance

to be enthroned \= action by which humans or deities assume a position of leadership, ◄ as if sitting on an actual throne

Space

to let (someone) stay, to make (someone) stay \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to remain in a place that is not their home for a relatively short time

[LEV 23:43](https://ref.ly/Lev23:43) · [1SA 30:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:21) · [PSA 143:3](https://ref.ly/Ps143:3) · [LAM 3:6](https://ref.ly/Lam3:6)

Space

to be set, to remain, to stay \= state in which an object has been placed in a certain location with the intention that it will stay there for an extended period of time

[PSA 122:5](https://ref.ly/Ps122:5) · [PSA 125:1](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1) · [SNG 5:12](https://ref.ly/Song5:12) · [JER 30:18](https://ref.ly/Jer30:18) · [NAM 3:8](https://ref.ly/Nah3:8) · [ZEC 12:6](https://ref.ly/Zech12:6) · [ZEC 14:10](https://ref.ly/Zech14:10)

Space

to set up \= causative action by which humans place an object in a certain location with the intention that it will stay there for an extended period of time

[EZK 25:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:4)

Dwell

to be settled, to be inhabited \= state in which a place or area has been made the home of humans

Dwell

to settle, to make (people) dwell \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to make a certain place or area their home

[GEN 47:6](https://ref.ly/Gen47:6) · [GEN 47:11](https://ref.ly/Gen47:11) · [1SA 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:8) · [2KI 17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:6) · [2KI 17:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:24) · [2KI 17:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:26) · [2CH 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:2) · [PSA 4:9](https://ref.ly/Ps4:9) · [PSA 68:7](https://ref.ly/Ps68:7) · [PSA 107:36](https://ref.ly/Ps107:36) · [PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9) · [ISA 5:8](https://ref.ly/Isa5:8) · [ISA 54:3](https://ref.ly/Isa54:3) · [EZK 26:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:20) · [EZK 36:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:11) · [HOS 11:11](https://ref.ly/Hos11:11) · [HOS 12:10](https://ref.ly/Hos12:10)

Dwell

to make inhabited \= causative action by which deities cause a certain place or area to become the home of humans

[EZK 36:33](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:33)

Dwell

settlement, dwelling \= a location that has been chosen by humans or deities as their dwelling place

[EXO 15:17](https://ref.ly/Exod15:17) · [NUM 21:15](https://ref.ly/Num21:15) · [1KI 8:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:30) · [1KI 8:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:39) · [1KI 8:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:43) · [1KI 8:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:49) · [2CH 6:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:21) · [2CH 6:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:30) · [2CH 6:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:33) · [2CH 6:39](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:39) · [PSA 68:17](https://ref.ly/Ps68:17) · [OBA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3)

Associate

to marry \= causative action by which humans brings other humans into their home as their spouse

[EZR 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:2) · [EZR 10:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:10) · [EZR 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:14) · [EZR 10:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:17) · [EZR 10:18](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:18) · [NEH 13:23](https://ref.ly/Neh13:23) · [NEH 13:27](https://ref.ly/Neh13:27)

Endure

to continue literally: to sit; hence: \= a process by which a certain condition persists over a relatively long period of time

[1KI 22:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:1) · [ZEC 1:11](https://ref.ly/Zech1:11)

Endure

to remain, to stay literally: to sit; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause a certain condition to persist over a relatively long period of time

## יְשׁוּעָה (id: 003156000000000)

יְשׁוּעָה
=========

יְשׁוּעָה (H3444) [SAHD](https://sahd-online.com/words/yshu3ah/)

noun f · ישׁע

Safe

success, salvation, deliverance, victory, protection \= causative action by which humans or deities come to the rescue or protection (of humans in need) implying success The word can denote various kinds of 'success' or 'blessing' secured by divine acts of power and faithfulness, cf. the SAHD article.

## יֶשַׁח (id: 003157000000000)

יֶשַׁח
======

יֶשַׁח (H3445)

noun m

Hunger

emptiness meaning unsure; possibly: \= state in which the human stomach is empty

[MIC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:14)1

1 MIC 6:14 The meaning of the phrase וְיֶשְׁחֲךָ בְּקִרְבֶּךָ is highly uncertain; among the suggestions are: RSV "and there shall be hunger in your inward parts" \| NIV "your stomach will still be empty" \| NJPS "And there is a gnawing at your vitals" \| REB "your food will lie heavy in your stomach" \| other meanings suggested for יֶשַׁח are "filth" (HALOT), "dysentery" or "faeces" or "semen" (DCH)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Micah 6:14

Asleep

to help, to deliver, to save \= causative action by which humans or deities come to the help of humans in need, whether in military, civil or judicial contexts

יֵשַׁע

## ישׁר (id: 003188000000000)

ישׁר
====

ישׁר (H3474)

verb · יָשָׁר · יֹשֶׁר · יִשְׁרָה · מִישֹׁור · מֵישָׁרִים · יְשֻׁרוּן

Shape

to make straight, to make smooth, to make level, to make even \= causative action by which humans or deities cause objects to be without curve or bend and/or have an even or regular surface

[1KI 6:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:35) · [ISA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3) · [ISA 45:2](https://ref.ly/Isa45:2)

Move

to go straight ahead \= action by which animals move forward without deviating to the left or the right

[1SA 6:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:12)

Move

**Collocation:** ישׁר אֹרַח

to make straight one's way \> to walk straight ahead literally: to make one's way straight; \= action by which humans move forward without deviating to the right or to the left; ≈ with the possible connotation of minding one's own affairs

[PRO 9:15](https://ref.ly/Prov9:15)

See

**Collocation:** ישׁר עַפְעַפַּיִם

to look straight ahead literally: to make straight one's eyelids; \= action by which humans look forward without being distracted by things visible on the left or on the right

[PRO 4:25](https://ref.ly/Prov4:25)

Right

**Collocation:** ישׁר אֹרַח · ישׁר דֶּרֶךְ

to make straight (someone's) way literally: to make someone else's way straight; \= causative action by which deities cause the actions and endeavors of humans to be prosperous and successful

[PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9) · [PRO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Prov3:6) · [PRO 11:5](https://ref.ly/Prov11:5) · [ISA 45:13](https://ref.ly/Isa45:13)

Right

to make straight (an event) \> to (perform an event) right literally: to make straight; \= causative action by which humans cause an action to be performed in a morally and ethically acceptable way

[PRO 15:21](https://ref.ly/Prov15:21)

Flow

to direct, to channel \= causative action by which humans cause water to flow in a certain direction

[2CH 32:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:30)

Innocent

to be upright, to be just \= state in which humans are morally and ethically right

[HAB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4)

Willing

**Collocation:** ישׁר בְּעֵינַיִם

to be straight in the eyes (of someone) \> to be accepted, to be approved, to please literally: to be straight in the eyes of someone; hence: \= state/process by which humans consider someone or something good

[NUM 23:27](https://ref.ly/Num23:27) · [JDG 14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:3) · [JDG 14:7](https://ref.ly/Judg14:7) · [1SA 18:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:20) · [1SA 18:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:26) · [2SA 17:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:4) · [1KI 9:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:12) · [1CH 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:4) · [2CH 30:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:4) · [JER 18:4](https://ref.ly/Jer18:4) · [JER 27:5](https://ref.ly/Jer27:5)

Willing

to consider right, to approve \= state/process by which humans consider something morally and ethically right

[PSA 119:128](https://ref.ly/Ps119:128)

1 ISA 45:2 MT\-K אושׁר \| MT\-Q אֲיַשֵּׁר 2 PSA 5:9 MT\-K הושׁר \| MT\-Q הַיְשַׁר

name

Names of People

noun f · ישׁר

## יֶתֶר (id: 003208000000000)

יֶתֶר
=====

יֶתֶר (H3499 · H3500 · H3501) — יִתְרָא

noun m · יתר

Remain

remainder, rest, remnant, survivor \= what remains in a certain place after part of the original quantity has disappeared or has been used or dealt with in a certain way; used for humans, animals, plants, and inanimate objects

Time

rest \= what remains in time after part of the original span of time has passed

[ISA 38:10](https://ref.ly/Isa38:10)

Happen

**Collocation:** יֶתֶר דְּבָרִים

rest (of one's acts) \= events that have taken place, that are not among a range of events that have been described or dealt with in a certain way; used for acts of kings only

Great

most important \= state in which humans have a higher status than others

[GEN 49:3](https://ref.ly/Gen49:3) · [GEN 49:3](https://ref.ly/Gen49:3)

Well

excellent, fine (words, speech) \= state in which human speech is of an excellent quality

Intense

more, exceedingly \= state in which an event is being done to a larger extent than is normally done

[PSA 31:24](https://ref.ly/Ps31:24) · [ISA 56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:12) · [DAN 8:9](https://ref.ly/Dan8:9)

Possess

possession, wealth literally: what remains, or is left over; hence: \= possessions that one has in surplus over the possession one needs in order to live

[JOB 22:20](https://ref.ly/Job22:20) · [PSA 17:14](https://ref.ly/Ps17:14)

noun m · מֵיתָר

Cords

cord, string \= relatively thin string; ► used to hold a tent or similar construction in place, but also as bowstring; in Judges 16 used to tie down a person

[JDG 16:7](https://ref.ly/Judg16:7) · [JDG 16:8](https://ref.ly/Judg16:8) · [JDG 16:9](https://ref.ly/Judg16:9) · [PSA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Ps11:2)

Dead

**Collocation:** נסע יֶתֶר

to pull up (someone's) tent\-cord \> to cause someone to die literally: to pull up (someone's) tent\-cord; hence: \= causative action by which an end is made to a human life ◄ as if it were a tent of which the cords are pulled up so that it will collapse

[JOB 4:21](https://ref.ly/Job4:21)

Weak

**Collocation:** פתח יֶתֶר

to loosen (someone's bowstring) \> to render (someone) weak, vulnerable literally: to loosen (someone's) bowstring; hence: \= causative action by which deities reduce the strength of humans and make them more vulnerable

[JOB 30:11](https://ref.ly/Job30:11)

name
